166 
167 struct TypeInfo {
168  uint64_t Width = 0;
169  unsigned Align = 0;
170  bool AlignIsRequired : 1;
171 
172  TypeInfo() : AlignIsRequired(false) {}
173  TypeInfo(uint64_t Width, unsigned Align, bool AlignIsRequired)
174  : Width(Width), Align(Align), AlignIsRequired(AlignIsRequired) {}
175 };
176 
177 struct TypeInfoChars {
178  CharUnits Width;
179  CharUnits Align;
180  bool AlignIsRequired : 1;
181 
182  TypeInfoChars() : AlignIsRequired(false) {}
183  TypeInfoChars(CharUnits Width, CharUnits Align, bool AlignIsRequired)
184  : Width(Width), Align(Align), AlignIsRequired(AlignIsRequired) {}
185 };
186 
187 /// Holds long-lived AST nodes (such as types and decls) that can be
188 /// referred to throughout the semantic analysis of a file.
189 class ASTContext : public RefCountedBase<ASTContext> {
190  friend class NestedNameSpecifier;
191 
192  mutable SmallVector<Type *, 0> Types;
193  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<ExtQuals> ExtQualNodes;
194  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<ComplexType> ComplexTypes;
195  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<PointerType> PointerTypes;
196  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<AdjustedType> AdjustedTypes;
197  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<BlockPointerType> BlockPointerTypes;
198  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<LValueReferenceType> LValueReferenceTypes;
199  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<RValueReferenceType> RValueReferenceTypes;
200  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<MemberPointerType> MemberPointerTypes;
201  mutable llvm::ContextualFoldingSet<ConstantArrayType, ASTContext &>
202  ConstantArrayTypes;
203  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<IncompleteArrayType> IncompleteArrayTypes;
204  mutable std::vector<VariableArrayType*> VariableArrayTypes;
205  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentSizedArrayType> DependentSizedArrayTypes;
206  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentSizedExtVectorType>
207  DependentSizedExtVectorTypes;
208  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentAddressSpaceType>
209  DependentAddressSpaceTypes;
210  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<VectorType> VectorTypes;
211  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentVectorType> DependentVectorTypes;
212  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<ConstantMatrixType> MatrixTypes;
213  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentSizedMatrixType> DependentSizedMatrixTypes;
214  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<FunctionNoProtoType> FunctionNoProtoTypes;
215  mutable llvm::ContextualFoldingSet<FunctionProtoType, ASTContext&>
216  FunctionProtoTypes;
217  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentTypeOfExprType> DependentTypeOfExprTypes;
218  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentDecltypeType> DependentDecltypeTypes;
219  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<TemplateTypeParmType> TemplateTypeParmTypes;
220  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<ObjCTypeParamType> ObjCTypeParamTypes;
221  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<SubstTemplateTypeParmType>
222  SubstTemplateTypeParmTypes;
223  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<SubstTemplateTypeParmPackType>
224  SubstTemplateTypeParmPackTypes;
225  mutable llvm::ContextualFoldingSet<TemplateSpecializationType, ASTContext&>
226  TemplateSpecializationTypes;
227  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<ParenType> ParenTypes;
228  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<ElaboratedType> ElaboratedTypes;
229  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentNameType> DependentNameTypes;
230  mutable llvm::ContextualFoldingSet<DependentTemplateSpecializationType,
231  ASTContext&>
232  DependentTemplateSpecializationTypes;
233  llvm::FoldingSet<PackExpansionType> PackExpansionTypes;
234  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<ObjCObjectTypeImpl> ObjCObjectTypes;
235  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<ObjCObjectPointerType> ObjCObjectPointerTypes;
236  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentUnaryTransformType>
237  DependentUnaryTransformTypes;
238  mutable llvm::ContextualFoldingSet<AutoType, ASTContext&> AutoTypes;
239  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DeducedTemplateSpecializationType>
240  DeducedTemplateSpecializationTypes;
241  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<AtomicType> AtomicTypes;
242  llvm::FoldingSet<AttributedType> AttributedTypes;
243  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<PipeType> PipeTypes;
244  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<ExtIntType> ExtIntTypes;
245  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentExtIntType> DependentExtIntTypes;
246 
247  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<QualifiedTemplateName> QualifiedTemplateNames;
248  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<DependentTemplateName> DependentTemplateNames;
249  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<SubstTemplateTemplateParmStorage>
250  SubstTemplateTemplateParms;
251  mutable llvm::ContextualFoldingSet<SubstTemplateTemplateParmPackStorage,
252  ASTContext&>
253  SubstTemplateTemplateParmPacks;
254 
255  /// The set of nested name specifiers.
256  ///
257  /// This set is managed by the NestedNameSpecifier class.
258  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<NestedNameSpecifier> NestedNameSpecifiers;
259  mutable NestedNameSpecifier *GlobalNestedNameSpecifier = nullptr;
260 
261  /// A cache mapping from RecordDecls to ASTRecordLayouts.
262  ///
263  /// This is lazily created. This is intentionally not serialized.
264  mutable llvm::DenseMap<const RecordDecl*, const ASTRecordLayout*>
265  ASTRecordLayouts;
266  mutable llvm::DenseMap<const ObjCContainerDecl*, const ASTRecordLayout*>
267  ObjCLayouts;
268 
269  /// A cache from types to size and alignment information.
270  using TypeInfoMap = llvm::DenseMap<const Type *, struct TypeInfo>;
271  mutable TypeInfoMap MemoizedTypeInfo;
272 
273  /// A cache from types to unadjusted alignment information. Only ARM and
274  /// AArch64 targets need this information, keeping it separate prevents
275  /// imposing overhead on TypeInfo size.
276  using UnadjustedAlignMap = llvm::DenseMap<const Type *, unsigned>;
277  mutable UnadjustedAlignMap MemoizedUnadjustedAlign;
278 
279  /// A cache mapping from CXXRecordDecls to key functions.
280  llvm::DenseMap<const CXXRecordDecl*, LazyDeclPtr> KeyFunctions;
281 
282  /// Mapping from ObjCContainers to their ObjCImplementations.
283  llvm::DenseMap<ObjCContainerDecl*, ObjCImplDecl*> ObjCImpls;
284 
285  /// Mapping from ObjCMethod to its duplicate declaration in the same
286  /// interface.
287  llvm::DenseMap<const ObjCMethodDecl*,const ObjCMethodDecl*> ObjCMethodRedecls;
288 
289  /// Mapping from __block VarDecls to BlockVarCopyInit.
290  llvm::DenseMap<const VarDecl *, BlockVarCopyInit> BlockVarCopyInits;
291 
292  /// Mapping from GUIDs to the corresponding MSGuidDecl.
293  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<MSGuidDecl> MSGuidDecls;
294 
295  /// Mapping from APValues to the corresponding TemplateParamObjects.
296  mutable llvm::FoldingSet<TemplateParamObjectDecl> TemplateParamObjectDecls;
297 
298  /// A cache mapping a string value to a StringLiteral object with the same
299  /// value.
300  ///
301  /// This is lazily created. This is intentionally not serialized.
302  mutable llvm::StringMap<StringLiteral *> StringLiteralCache;
303 
304  /// MD5 hash of CUID. It is calculated when first used and cached by this
305  /// data member.
306  mutable std::string CUIDHash;
307 
308  /// Representation of a "canonical" template template parameter that
309  /// is used in canonical template names.
310  class CanonicalTemplateTemplateParm : public llvm::FoldingSetNode {
311  TemplateTemplateParmDecl *Parm;
312 
313  public:
314  CanonicalTemplateTemplateParm(TemplateTemplateParmDecl *Parm)
315  : Parm(Parm) {}
316 
317  TemplateTemplateParmDecl *getParam() const { return Parm; }
318 
319  void Profile(llvm::FoldingSetNodeID &ID, const ASTContext &C) {
320  Profile(ID, C, Parm);
321  }
322 
323  static void Profile(llvm::FoldingSetNodeID &ID,
324  const ASTContext &C,
325  TemplateTemplateParmDecl *Parm);
326  };
327  mutable llvm::ContextualFoldingSet<CanonicalTemplateTemplateParm,
328  const ASTContext&>
329  CanonTemplateTemplateParms;
330 
331  TemplateTemplateParmDecl *
332  getCanonicalTemplateTemplateParmDecl(TemplateTemplateParmDecl *TTP) const;
333 
334  /// The typedef for the __int128_t type.
335  mutable TypedefDecl *Int128Decl = nullptr;
336 
337  /// The typedef for the __uint128_t type.
338  mutable TypedefDecl *UInt128Decl = nullptr;
339 
340  /// The typedef for the target specific predefined
341  /// __builtin_va_list type.
342  mutable TypedefDecl *BuiltinVaListDecl = nullptr;
343 
344  /// The typedef for the predefined \c __builtin_ms_va_list type.
345  mutable TypedefDecl *BuiltinMSVaListDecl = nullptr;
346 
347  /// The typedef for the predefined \c id type.
348  mutable TypedefDecl *ObjCIdDecl = nullptr;
349 
350  /// The typedef for the predefined \c SEL type.
351  mutable TypedefDecl *ObjCSelDecl = nullptr;
352 
353  /// The typedef for the predefined \c Class type.
354  mutable TypedefDecl *ObjCClassDecl = nullptr;
355 
356  /// The typedef for the predefined \c Protocol class in Objective-C.
357  mutable ObjCInterfaceDecl *ObjCProtocolClassDecl = nullptr;
358 
359  /// The typedef for the predefined 'BOOL' type.
360  mutable TypedefDecl *BOOLDecl = nullptr;
361 
362  // Typedefs which may be provided defining the structure of Objective-C
363  // pseudo-builtins
364  QualType ObjCIdRedefinitionType;
365  QualType ObjCClassRedefinitionType;
366  QualType ObjCSelRedefinitionType;
367 
368  /// The identifier 'bool'.
369  mutable IdentifierInfo *BoolName = nullptr;
370 
371  /// The identifier 'NSObject'.
372  mutable IdentifierInfo *NSObjectName = nullptr;
373 
374  /// The identifier 'NSCopying'.
375  IdentifierInfo *NSCopyingName = nullptr;
376 
377  /// The identifier '__make_integer_seq'.
378  mutable IdentifierInfo *MakeIntegerSeqName = nullptr;
379 
380  /// The identifier '__type_pack_element'.
381  mutable IdentifierInfo *TypePackElementName = nullptr;
382 
383  QualType ObjCConstantStringType;
384  mutable RecordDecl *CFConstantStringTagDecl = nullptr;
385  mutable TypedefDecl *CFConstantStringTypeDecl = nullptr;
386 
387  mutable QualType ObjCSuperType;
388 
389  QualType ObjCNSStringType;
390 
391  /// The typedef declaration for the Objective-C "instancetype" type.
392  TypedefDecl *ObjCInstanceTypeDecl = nullptr;
393 
394  /// The type for the C FILE type.
395  TypeDecl *FILEDecl = nullptr;
396 
397  /// The type for the C jmp_buf type.
398  TypeDecl *jmp_bufDecl = nullptr;
399 
400  /// The type for the C sigjmp_buf type.
401  TypeDecl *sigjmp_bufDecl = nullptr;
402 
403  /// The type for the C ucontext_t type.
404  TypeDecl *ucontext_tDecl = nullptr;
405 
406  /// Type for the Block descriptor for Blocks CodeGen.
407  ///
408  /// Since this is only used for generation of debug info, it is not
409  /// serialized.
410  mutable RecordDecl *BlockDescriptorType = nullptr;
411 
412  /// Type for the Block descriptor for Blocks CodeGen.
413  ///
414  /// Since this is only used for generation of debug info, it is not
415  /// serialized.
416  mutable RecordDecl *BlockDescriptorExtendedType = nullptr;
417 
418  /// Declaration for the CUDA cudaConfigureCall function.
419  FunctionDecl *cudaConfigureCallDecl = nullptr;
420 
421  /// Keeps track of all declaration attributes.
422  ///
423  /// Since so few decls have attrs, we keep them in a hash map instead of
424  /// wasting space in the Decl class.
425  llvm::DenseMap<const Decl*, AttrVec*> DeclAttrs;
426 
427  /// A mapping from non-redeclarable declarations in modules that were
428  /// merged with other declarations to the canonical declaration that they were
429  /// merged into.
430  llvm::DenseMap<Decl*, Decl*> MergedDecls;
431 
432  /// A mapping from a defining declaration to a list of modules (other
433  /// than the owning module of the declaration) that contain merged
434  /// definitions of that entity.
435  llvm::DenseMap<NamedDecl*, llvm::TinyPtrVector<Module*>> MergedDefModules;
436 
437  /// Initializers for a module, in order. Each Decl will be either
438  /// something that has a semantic effect on startup (such as a variable with
439  /// a non-constant initializer), or an ImportDecl (which recursively triggers
440  /// initialization of another module).
441  struct PerModuleInitializers {
442  llvm::SmallVector<Decl*, 4> Initializers;
443  llvm::SmallVector<uint32_t, 4> LazyInitializers;
444 
445  void resolve(ASTContext &Ctx);
446  };
447  llvm::DenseMap<Module*, PerModuleInitializers*> ModuleInitializers;
448 
449  ASTContext &this_() { return *this; }
450 
451 public:
452  /// A type synonym for the TemplateOrInstantiation mapping.
453  using TemplateOrSpecializationInfo =
454  llvm::PointerUnion<VarTemplateDecl *, MemberSpecializationInfo *>;
455 
456 private:
457  friend class ASTDeclReader;
458  friend class ASTReader;
459  friend class ASTWriter;
460  template <class> friend class serialization::AbstractTypeReader;
461  friend class CXXRecordDecl;
462 
463  /// A mapping to contain the template or declaration that
464  /// a variable declaration describes or was instantiated from,
465  /// respectively.
466  ///
467  /// For non-templates, this value will be NULL. For variable
468  /// declarations that describe a variable template, this will be a
469  /// pointer to a VarTemplateDecl. For static data members
470  /// of class template specializations, this will be the
471  /// MemberSpecializationInfo referring to the member variable that was
472  /// instantiated or specialized. Thus, the mapping will keep track of
473  /// the static data member templates from which static data members of
474  /// class template specializations were instantiated.
475  ///
476  /// Given the following example:
477  ///
478  /// \code
479  /// template<typename T>
480  /// struct X {
481  /// static T value;
482  /// };
483  ///
484  /// template<typename T>
485  /// T X<T>::value = T(17);
486  ///
487  /// int *x = &X<int>::value;
488  /// \endcode
489  ///
490  /// This mapping will contain an entry that maps from the VarDecl for
491  /// X<int>::value to the corresponding VarDecl for X<T>::value (within the
492  /// class template X) and will be marked TSK_ImplicitInstantiation.
493  llvm::DenseMap<const VarDecl *, TemplateOrSpecializationInfo>
494  TemplateOrInstantiation;
495 
496  /// Keeps track of the declaration from which a using declaration was
497  /// created during instantiation.
498  ///
499  /// The source and target declarations are always a UsingDecl, an
500  /// UnresolvedUsingValueDecl, or an UnresolvedUsingTypenameDecl.
501  ///
502  /// For example:
503  /// \code
504  /// template<typename T>
505  /// struct A {
506  /// void f();
507  /// };
508  ///
509  /// template<typename T>
510  /// struct B : A<T> {
511  /// using A<T>::f;
512  /// };
513  ///
514  /// template struct B<int>;
515  /// \endcode
516  ///
517  /// This mapping will contain an entry that maps from the UsingDecl in
518  /// B<int> to the UnresolvedUsingDecl in B<T>.
519  llvm::DenseMap<NamedDecl *, NamedDecl *> InstantiatedFromUsingDecl;
520 
521  /// Like InstantiatedFromUsingDecl, but for using-enum-declarations. Maps
522  /// from the instantiated using-enum to the templated decl from whence it
523  /// came.
524  /// Note that using-enum-declarations cannot be dependent and
525  /// thus will never be instantiated from an "unresolved"
526  /// version thereof (as with using-declarations), so each mapping is from
527  /// a (resolved) UsingEnumDecl to a (resolved) UsingEnumDecl.
528  llvm::DenseMap<UsingEnumDecl *, UsingEnumDecl *>
529  InstantiatedFromUsingEnumDecl;
530 
531  /// Simlarly maps instantiated UsingShadowDecls to their origin.
532  llvm::DenseMap<UsingShadowDecl*, UsingShadowDecl*>
533  InstantiatedFromUsingShadowDecl;
534 
535  llvm::DenseMap<FieldDecl *, FieldDecl *> InstantiatedFromUnnamedFieldDecl;
536 
537  /// Mapping that stores the methods overridden by a given C++
538  /// member function.
539  ///
540  /// Since most C++ member functions aren't virtual and therefore
541  /// don't override anything, we store the overridden functions in
542  /// this map on the side rather than within the CXXMethodDecl structure.
543  using CXXMethodVector = llvm::TinyPtrVector<const CXXMethodDecl *>;
544  llvm::DenseMap<const CXXMethodDecl *, CXXMethodVector> OverriddenMethods;
545 
546  /// Mapping from each declaration context to its corresponding
547  /// mangling numbering context (used for constructs like lambdas which
548  /// need to be consistently numbered for the mangler).
549  llvm::DenseMap<const DeclContext *, std::unique_ptr<MangleNumberingContext>>
550  MangleNumberingContexts;
551  llvm::DenseMap<const Decl *, std::unique_ptr<MangleNumberingContext>>
552  ExtraMangleNumberingContexts;
553 
554  /// Side-table of mangling numbers for declarations which rarely
555  /// need them (like static local vars).
556  llvm::MapVector<const NamedDecl *, unsigned> MangleNumbers;
557  llvm::MapVector<const VarDecl *, unsigned> StaticLocalNumbers;
558  /// Mapping the associated device lambda mangling number if present.
559  mutable llvm::DenseMap<const CXXRecordDecl *, unsigned>
560  DeviceLambdaManglingNumbers;
561 
562  /// Mapping that stores parameterIndex values for ParmVarDecls when
563  /// that value exceeds the bitfield size of ParmVarDeclBits.ParameterIndex.
564  using ParameterIndexTable = llvm::DenseMap<const VarDecl *, unsigned>;
565  ParameterIndexTable ParamIndices;
566 
567  ImportDecl *FirstLocalImport = nullptr;
568  ImportDecl *LastLocalImport = nullptr;
569 
570  TranslationUnitDecl *TUDecl;
571  mutable ExternCContextDecl *ExternCContext = nullptr;
572  mutable BuiltinTemplateDecl *MakeIntegerSeqDecl = nullptr;
573  mutable BuiltinTemplateDecl *TypePackElementDecl = nullptr;
574 
575  /// The associated SourceManager object.
576  SourceManager &SourceMgr;
577 
578  /// The language options used to create the AST associated with
579  /// this ASTContext object.
580  LangOptions &LangOpts;
581 
582  /// NoSanitizeList object that is used by sanitizers to decide which
583  /// entities should not be instrumented.
584  std::unique_ptr<NoSanitizeList> NoSanitizeL;
585 
586  /// Function filtering mechanism to determine whether a given function
587  /// should be imbued with the XRay "always" or "never" attributes.
588  std::unique_ptr<XRayFunctionFilter> XRayFilter;
589 
590  /// ProfileList object that is used by the profile instrumentation
591  /// to decide which entities should be instrumented.
592  std::unique_ptr<ProfileList> ProfList;
593 
594  /// The allocator used to create AST objects.
595  ///
596  /// AST objects are never destructed; rather, all memory associated with the
597  /// AST objects will be released when the ASTContext itself is destroyed.
598  mutable llvm::BumpPtrAllocator BumpAlloc;
599 
600  /// Allocator for partial diagnostics.
601  PartialDiagnostic::DiagStorageAllocator DiagAllocator;
602 
603  /// The current C++ ABI.
604  std::unique_ptr<CXXABI> ABI;
605  CXXABI *createCXXABI(const TargetInfo &T);
606 
607  /// The logical -> physical address space map.
608  const LangASMap *AddrSpaceMap = nullptr;
609 
610  /// Address space map mangling must be used with language specific
611  /// address spaces (e.g. OpenCL/CUDA)
612  bool AddrSpaceMapMangling;
613 
614  const TargetInfo *Target = nullptr;
615  const TargetInfo *AuxTarget = nullptr;
616  clang::PrintingPolicy PrintingPolicy;
617  std::unique_ptr<interp::Context> InterpContext;
618  std::unique_ptr<ParentMapContext> ParentMapCtx;
619 
620  /// Keeps track of the deallocated DeclListNodes for future reuse.
621  DeclListNode *ListNodeFreeList = nullptr;
622 
623 public:
624  IdentifierTable &Idents;
625  SelectorTable &Selectors;
626  Builtin::Context &BuiltinInfo;
627  mutable DeclarationNameTable DeclarationNames;
628  IntrusiveRefCntPtr<ExternalASTSource> ExternalSource;
629  ASTMutationListener *Listener = nullptr;
630 
631  /// Returns the clang bytecode interpreter context.
632  interp::Context &getInterpContext();
633 
634  /// Returns the dynamic AST node parent map context.
635  ParentMapContext &getParentMapContext();
636 
637  // A traversal scope limits the parts of the AST visible to certain analyses.
638  // RecursiveASTVisitor only visits specified children of TranslationUnitDecl.
639  // getParents() will only observe reachable parent edges.
640  //
641  // The scope is defined by a set of "top-level" declarations which will be
642  // visible under the TranslationUnitDecl.
643  // Initially, it is the entire TU, represented by {getTranslationUnitDecl()}.
644  //
645  // After setTraversalScope({foo, bar}), the exposed AST looks like:
646  // TranslationUnitDecl
647  // - foo
648  // - ...
649  // - bar
650  // - ...
651  // All other siblings of foo and bar are pruned from the tree.
652  // (However they are still accessible via TranslationUnitDecl->decls())
653  //
654  // Changing the scope clears the parent cache, which is expensive to rebuild.
655  std::vector<Decl *> getTraversalScope() const { return TraversalScope; }
656  void setTraversalScope(const std::vector<Decl *> &);
657 
658  /// Forwards to get node parents from the ParentMapContext. New callers should
659  /// use ParentMapContext::getParents() directly.
660  template <typename NodeT> DynTypedNodeList getParents(const NodeT &Node);
661 
662  const clang::PrintingPolicy &getPrintingPolicy() const {
663  return PrintingPolicy;
664  }
665 
666  void setPrintingPolicy(const clang::PrintingPolicy &Policy) {
667  PrintingPolicy = Policy;
668  }
669 
670  SourceManager& getSourceManager() { return SourceMgr; }
671  const SourceManager& getSourceManager() const { return SourceMgr; }
672 
673  llvm::BumpPtrAllocator &getAllocator() const {
674  return BumpAlloc;
675  }
676 
677  void *Allocate(size_t Size, unsigned Align = 8) const {
678  return BumpAlloc.Allocate(Size, Align);
679  }
680  template <typename T> T *Allocate(size_t Num = 1) const {
681  return static_cast<T *>(Allocate(Num * sizeof(T), alignof(T)));
682  }
683  void Deallocate(void *Ptr) const {}
684 
685  /// Allocates a \c DeclListNode or returns one from the \c ListNodeFreeList
686  /// pool.
687  DeclListNode *AllocateDeclListNode(clang::NamedDecl *ND) {
688  if (DeclListNode *Alloc = ListNodeFreeList) {
689  ListNodeFreeList = Alloc->Rest.dyn_cast<DeclListNode*>();
690  Alloc->D = ND;
691  Alloc->Rest = nullptr;
692  return Alloc;
693  }
694  return new (*this) DeclListNode(ND);
695  }
696  /// Deallcates a \c DeclListNode by returning it to the \c ListNodeFreeList
697  /// pool.
698  void DeallocateDeclListNode(DeclListNode *N) {
699  N->Rest = ListNodeFreeList;
700  ListNodeFreeList = N;
701  }
702 
703  /// Return the total amount of physical memory allocated for representing
704  /// AST nodes and type information.
705  size_t getASTAllocatedMemory() const {
706  return BumpAlloc.getTotalMemory();
707  }
708 
709  /// Return the total memory used for various side tables.
710  size_t getSideTableAllocatedMemory() const;
711 
712  PartialDiagnostic::DiagStorageAllocator &getDiagAllocator() {
713  return DiagAllocator;
714  }
715 
716  const TargetInfo &getTargetInfo() const { return *Target; }
717  const TargetInfo *getAuxTargetInfo() const { return AuxTarget; }
718 
719  /// getIntTypeForBitwidth -
720  /// sets integer QualTy according to specified details:
721  /// bitwidth, signed/unsigned.
722  /// Returns empty type if there is no appropriate target types.
723  QualType getIntTypeForBitwidth(unsigned DestWidth,
724  unsigned Signed) const;
725 
726  /// getRealTypeForBitwidth -
727  /// sets floating point QualTy according to specified bitwidth.
728  /// Returns empty type if there is no appropriate target types.
729  QualType getRealTypeForBitwidth(unsigned DestWidth, bool ExplicitIEEE) const;
730 
731  bool AtomicUsesUnsupportedLibcall(const AtomicExpr *E) const;
732 
733  const LangOptions& getLangOpts() const { return LangOpts; }
734 
735  // If this condition is false, typo correction must be performed eagerly
736  // rather than delayed in many places, as it makes use of dependent types.
737  // the condition is false for clang's C-only codepath, as it doesn't support
738  // dependent types yet.
739  bool isDependenceAllowed() const {
740  return LangOpts.CPlusPlus || LangOpts.RecoveryAST;
741  }
742 
743  const NoSanitizeList &getNoSanitizeList() const { return *NoSanitizeL; }
744 
745  const XRayFunctionFilter &getXRayFilter() const {
746  return *XRayFilter;
747  }
748 
749  const ProfileList &getProfileList() const { return *ProfList; }
750 
751  DiagnosticsEngine &getDiagnostics() const;
752 
753  FullSourceLoc getFullLoc(SourceLocation Loc) const {
754  return FullSourceLoc(Loc,SourceMgr);
755  }
756 
757  /// Return the C++ ABI kind that should be used. The C++ ABI can be overriden
758  /// at compile time with `-fc++-abi=`. If this is not provided, we instead use
759  /// the default ABI set by the target.
760  TargetCXXABI::Kind getCXXABIKind() const;
761 
762  /// All comments in this translation unit.
763  RawCommentList Comments;
764 
765  /// True if comments are already loaded from ExternalASTSource.
766  mutable bool CommentsLoaded = false;
767 
768  /// Mapping from declaration to directly attached comment.
769  ///
770  /// Raw comments are owned by Comments list. This mapping is populated
771  /// lazily.
772  mutable llvm::DenseMap<const Decl *, const RawComment *> DeclRawComments;
773 
774  /// Mapping from canonical declaration to the first redeclaration in chain
775  /// that has a comment attached.
776  ///
777  /// Raw comments are owned by Comments list. This mapping is populated
778  /// lazily.
779  mutable llvm::DenseMap<const Decl *, const Decl *> RedeclChainComments;
780 
781  /// Keeps track of redeclaration chains that don't have any comment attached.
782  /// Mapping from canonical declaration to redeclaration chain that has no
783  /// comments attached to any redeclaration. Specifically it's mapping to
784  /// the last redeclaration we've checked.
785  ///
786  /// Shall not contain declarations that have comments attached to any
787  /// redeclaration in their chain.
788  mutable llvm::DenseMap<const Decl *, const Decl *> CommentlessRedeclChains;
789 
790  /// Mapping from declarations to parsed comments attached to any
791  /// redeclaration.
792  mutable llvm::DenseMap<const Decl *, comments::FullComment *> ParsedComments;
793 
794  /// Attaches \p Comment to \p OriginalD and to its redeclaration chain
795  /// and removes the redeclaration chain from the set of commentless chains.
796  ///
797  /// Don't do anything if a comment has already been attached to \p OriginalD
798  /// or its redeclaration chain.
799  void cacheRawCommentForDecl(const Decl &OriginalD,
800  const RawComment &Comment) const;
801 
802  /// \returns searches \p CommentsInFile for doc comment for \p D.
803  ///
804  /// \p RepresentativeLocForDecl is used as a location for searching doc
805  /// comments. \p CommentsInFile is a mapping offset -> comment of files in the
806  /// same file where \p RepresentativeLocForDecl is.
807  RawComment *getRawCommentForDeclNoCacheImpl(
808  const Decl *D, const SourceLocation RepresentativeLocForDecl,
809  const std::map<unsigned, RawComment *> &CommentsInFile) const;
810 
811  /// Return the documentation comment attached to a given declaration,
812  /// without looking into cache.
813  RawComment *getRawCommentForDeclNoCache(const Decl *D) const;
814 
815 public:
816  void addComment(const RawComment &RC);
817 
818  /// Return the documentation comment attached to a given declaration.
819  /// Returns nullptr if no comment is attached.
820  ///
821  /// \param OriginalDecl if not nullptr, is set to declaration AST node that
822  /// had the comment, if the comment we found comes from a redeclaration.
823  const RawComment *
824  getRawCommentForAnyRedecl(const Decl *D,
825  const Decl **OriginalDecl = nullptr) const;
826 
827  /// Searches existing comments for doc comments that should be attached to \p
828  /// Decls. If any doc comment is found, it is parsed.
829  ///
830  /// Requirement: All \p Decls are in the same file.
831  ///
832  /// If the last comment in the file is already attached we assume
833  /// there are not comments left to be attached to \p Decls.
834  void attachCommentsToJustParsedDecls(ArrayRef<Decl *> Decls,
835  const Preprocessor *PP);
836 
837  /// Return parsed documentation comment attached to a given declaration.
838  /// Returns nullptr if no comment is attached.
839  ///
840  /// \param PP the Preprocessor used with this TU. Could be nullptr if
841  /// preprocessor is not available.
842  comments::FullComment *getCommentForDecl(const Decl *D,
843  const Preprocessor *PP) const;
844 
845  /// Return parsed documentation comment attached to a given declaration.
846  /// Returns nullptr if no comment is attached. Does not look at any
847  /// redeclarations of the declaration.
848  comments::FullComment *getLocalCommentForDeclUncached(const Decl *D) const;
849 
850  comments::FullComment *cloneFullComment(comments::FullComment *FC,
851  const Decl *D) const;
852 
853 private:
854  mutable comments::CommandTraits CommentCommandTraits;
855 
856  /// Iterator that visits import declarations.
857  class import_iterator {
858  ImportDecl *Import = nullptr;
859 
860  public:
861  using value_type = ImportDecl *;
862  using reference = ImportDecl *;
863  using pointer = ImportDecl *;
864  using difference_type = int;
865  using iterator_category = std::forward_iterator_tag;
866 
867  import_iterator() = default;
868  explicit import_iterator(ImportDecl *Import) : Import(Import) {}
869 
870  reference operator*() const { return Import; }
871  pointer operator->() const { return Import; }
872 
873  import_iterator &operator++() {
874  Import = ASTContext::getNextLocalImport(Import);
875  return *this;
876  }
877 
878  import_iterator operator++(int) {
879  import_iterator Other(*this);
880  ++(*this);
881  return Other;
882  }
883 
884  friend bool operator==(import_iterator X, import_iterator Y) {
885  return X.Import == Y.Import;
886  }
887 
888  friend bool operator!=(import_iterator X, import_iterator Y) {
889  return X.Import != Y.Import;
890  }
891  };
892 
893 public:
894  comments::CommandTraits &getCommentCommandTraits() const {
895  return CommentCommandTraits;
896  }
897 
898  /// Retrieve the attributes for the given declaration.
899  AttrVec& getDeclAttrs(const Decl *D);
900 
901  /// Erase the attributes corresponding to the given declaration.
902  void eraseDeclAttrs(const Decl *D);
903 
904  /// If this variable is an instantiated static data member of a
905  /// class template specialization, returns the templated static data member
906  /// from which it was instantiated.
907  // FIXME: Remove ?
908  MemberSpecializationInfo *getInstantiatedFromStaticDataMember(
909  const VarDecl *Var);
910 
911  /// Note that the static data member \p Inst is an instantiation of
912  /// the static data member template \p Tmpl of a class template.
913  void setInstantiatedFromStaticDataMember(VarDecl *Inst, VarDecl *Tmpl,
914  TemplateSpecializationKind TSK,
915  SourceLocation PointOfInstantiation = SourceLocation());
916 
917  TemplateOrSpecializationInfo
918  getTemplateOrSpecializationInfo(const VarDecl *Var);
919 
920  void setTemplateOrSpecializationInfo(VarDecl *Inst,
921  TemplateOrSpecializationInfo TSI);
922 
923  /// If the given using decl \p Inst is an instantiation of
924  /// another (possibly unresolved) using decl, return it.
925  NamedDecl *getInstantiatedFromUsingDecl(NamedDecl *Inst);
926 
927  /// Remember that the using decl \p Inst is an instantiation
928  /// of the using decl \p Pattern of a class template.
929  void setInstantiatedFromUsingDecl(NamedDecl *Inst, NamedDecl *Pattern);
930 
931  /// If the given using-enum decl \p Inst is an instantiation of
932  /// another using-enum decl, return it.
933  UsingEnumDecl *getInstantiatedFromUsingEnumDecl(UsingEnumDecl *Inst);
934 
935  /// Remember that the using enum decl \p Inst is an instantiation
936  /// of the using enum decl \p Pattern of a class template.
937  void setInstantiatedFromUsingEnumDecl(UsingEnumDecl *Inst,
938  UsingEnumDecl *Pattern);
939 
940  UsingShadowDecl *getInstantiatedFromUsingShadowDecl(UsingShadowDecl *Inst);
941  void setInstantiatedFromUsingShadowDecl(UsingShadowDecl *Inst,
942  UsingShadowDecl *Pattern);
943 
944  FieldDecl *getInstantiatedFromUnnamedFieldDecl(FieldDecl *Field);
945 
946  void setInstantiatedFromUnnamedFieldDecl(FieldDecl *Inst, FieldDecl *Tmpl);
947 
948  // Access to the set of methods overridden by the given C++ method.
949  using overridden_cxx_method_iterator = CXXMethodVector::const_iterator;
950  overridden_cxx_method_iterator
951  overridden_methods_begin(const CXXMethodDecl *Method) const;
952 
953  overridden_cxx_method_iterator
954  overridden_methods_end(const CXXMethodDecl *Method) const;
955 
956  unsigned overridden_methods_size(const CXXMethodDecl *Method) const;
957 
958  using overridden_method_range =
959  llvm::iterator_range<overridden_cxx_method_iterator>;
960 
961  overridden_method_range overridden_methods(const CXXMethodDecl *Method) const;
962 
963  /// Note that the given C++ \p Method overrides the given \p
964  /// Overridden method.
965  void addOverriddenMethod(const CXXMethodDecl *Method,
966  const CXXMethodDecl *Overridden);
967 
968  /// Return C++ or ObjC overridden methods for the given \p Method.
969  ///
970  /// An ObjC method is considered to override any method in the class's
971  /// base classes, its protocols, or its categories' protocols, that has
972  /// the same selector and is of the same kind (class or instance).
973  /// A method in an implementation is not considered as overriding the same
974  /// method in the interface or its categories.
975  void getOverriddenMethods(
976  const NamedDecl *Method,
977  SmallVectorImpl<const NamedDecl *> &Overridden) const;
978 
979  /// Notify the AST context that a new import declaration has been
980  /// parsed or implicitly created within this translation unit.
981  void addedLocalImportDecl(ImportDecl *Import);
982 
983  static ImportDecl *getNextLocalImport(ImportDecl *Import) {
984  return Import->getNextLocalImport();
985  }
986 
987  using import_range = llvm::iterator_range<import_iterator>;
988 
989  import_range local_imports() const {
990  return import_range(import_iterator(FirstLocalImport), import_iterator());
991  }
992 
993  Decl *getPrimaryMergedDecl(Decl *D) {
994  Decl *Result = MergedDecls.lookup(D);
995  return Result ? Result : D;
996  }
997  void setPrimaryMergedDecl(Decl *D, Decl *Primary) {
998  MergedDecls[D] = Primary;
999  }
1000 
1001  /// Note that the definition \p ND has been merged into module \p M,
1002  /// and should be visible whenever \p M is visible.
1003  void mergeDefinitionIntoModule(NamedDecl *ND, Module *M,
1004  bool NotifyListeners = true);
1005 
1006  /// Clean up the merged definition list. Call this if you might have
1007  /// added duplicates into the list.
1008  void deduplicateMergedDefinitonsFor(NamedDecl *ND);
1009 
1010  /// Get the additional modules in which the definition \p Def has
1011  /// been merged.
1012  ArrayRef<Module*> getModulesWithMergedDefinition(const NamedDecl *Def);
1013 
1014  /// Add a declaration to the list of declarations that are initialized
1015  /// for a module. This will typically be a global variable (with internal
1016  /// linkage) that runs module initializers, such as the iostream initializer,
1017  /// or an ImportDecl nominating another module that has initializers.
1018  void addModuleInitializer(Module *M, Decl *Init);
1019 
1020  void addLazyModuleInitializers(Module *M, ArrayRef<uint32_t> IDs);
1021 
1022  /// Get the initializations to perform when importing a module, if any.
1023  ArrayRef<Decl*> getModuleInitializers(Module *M);
1024 
1025  TranslationUnitDecl *getTranslationUnitDecl() const { return TUDecl; }
1026 
1027  ExternCContextDecl *getExternCContextDecl() const;
1028  BuiltinTemplateDecl *getMakeIntegerSeqDecl() const;
1029  BuiltinTemplateDecl *getTypePackElementDecl() const;
1030 
1031  // Builtin Types.
1032  CanQualType VoidTy;
1033  CanQualType BoolTy;
1034  CanQualType CharTy;
1035  CanQualType WCharTy; // [C++ 3.9.1p5].
1036  CanQualType WideCharTy; // Same as WCharTy in C++, integer type in C99.
1037  CanQualType WIntTy; // [C99 7.24.1], integer type unchanged by default promotions.
1038  CanQualType Char8Ty; // [C++20 proposal]
1039  CanQualType Char16Ty; // [C++0x 3.9.1p5], integer type in C99.
1040  CanQualType Char32Ty; // [C++0x 3.9.1p5], integer type in C99.
1041  CanQualType SignedCharTy, ShortTy, IntTy, LongTy, LongLongTy, Int128Ty;
1042  CanQualType UnsignedCharTy, UnsignedShortTy, UnsignedIntTy, UnsignedLongTy;
1043  CanQualType UnsignedLongLongTy, UnsignedInt128Ty;
1044  CanQualType FloatTy, DoubleTy, LongDoubleTy, Float128Ty;
1045  CanQualType ShortAccumTy, AccumTy,
1046  LongAccumTy; // ISO/IEC JTC1 SC22 WG14 N1169 Extension
1047  CanQualType UnsignedShortAccumTy, UnsignedAccumTy, UnsignedLongAccumTy;
1048  CanQualType ShortFractTy, FractTy, LongFractTy;
1049  CanQualType UnsignedShortFractTy, UnsignedFractTy, UnsignedLongFractTy;
1050  CanQualType SatShortAccumTy, SatAccumTy, SatLongAccumTy;
1051  CanQualType SatUnsignedShortAccumTy, SatUnsignedAccumTy,
1052  SatUnsignedLongAccumTy;
1053  CanQualType SatShortFractTy, SatFractTy, SatLongFractTy;
1054  CanQualType SatUnsignedShortFractTy, SatUnsignedFractTy,
1055  SatUnsignedLongFractTy;
1056  CanQualType HalfTy; // [OpenCL 6.1.1.1], ARM NEON
1057  CanQualType BFloat16Ty;
1058  CanQualType Float16Ty; // C11 extension ISO/IEC TS 18661-3
1059  CanQualType FloatComplexTy, DoubleComplexTy, LongDoubleComplexTy;
1060  CanQualType Float128ComplexTy;
1061  CanQualType VoidPtrTy, NullPtrTy;
1062  CanQualType DependentTy, OverloadTy, BoundMemberTy, UnknownAnyTy;
1063  CanQualType BuiltinFnTy;
1064  CanQualType PseudoObjectTy, ARCUnbridgedCastTy;
1065  CanQualType ObjCBuiltinIdTy, ObjCBuiltinClassTy, ObjCBuiltinSelTy;
1066  CanQualType ObjCBuiltinBoolTy;
1067 #define IMAGE_TYPE(ImgType, Id, SingletonId, Access, Suffix) \
1068  CanQualType SingletonId;
1069 #include "clang/Basic/OpenCLImageTypes.def"
1070 #define IMAGE_TYPE(ImgType, Id, SingletonId, Access, Suffix) \
1071  CanQualType Sampled##SingletonId;
1072 #define IMAGE_WRITE_TYPE(Type, Id, Ext)
1073 #define IMAGE_READ_WRITE_TYPE(Type, Id, Ext)
1074 #include "clang/Basic/OpenCLImageTypes.def"
1075  CanQualType OCLSamplerTy, OCLEventTy, OCLClkEventTy;
1076  CanQualType OCLQueueTy, OCLReserveIDTy;
1077  CanQualType IncompleteMatrixIdxTy;
1078  CanQualType OMPArraySectionTy, OMPArrayShapingTy, OMPIteratorTy;
1079 #define EXT_OPAQUE_TYPE(ExtType, Id, Ext) \
1080  CanQualType Id##Ty;
1081 #include "clang/Basic/OpenCLExtensionTypes.def"
1082 #define SVE_TYPE(Name, Id, SingletonId) \
1083  CanQualType SingletonId;
1084 #include "clang/Basic/AArch64SVEACLETypes.def"
1085 #define PPC_VECTOR_TYPE(Name, Id, Size) \
1086  CanQualType Id##Ty;
1087 #include "clang/Basic/PPCTypes.def"
1088 #define RVV_TYPE(Name, Id, SingletonId) \
1089  CanQualType SingletonId;
1090 #include "clang/Basic/RISCVVTypes.def"
1091 
1092  // Types for deductions in C++0x [stmt.ranged]'s desugaring. Built on demand.
1093  mutable QualType AutoDeductTy; // Deduction against 'auto'.
1094  mutable QualType AutoRRefDeductTy; // Deduction against 'auto &&'.
1095 
1096  // Decl used to help define __builtin_va_list for some targets.
1097  // The decl is built when constructing 'BuiltinVaListDecl'.
1098  mutable Decl *VaListTagDecl = nullptr;
1099 
1100  // Implicitly-declared type 'struct _GUID'.
1101  mutable TagDecl *MSGuidTagDecl = nullptr;
1102 
1103  /// Keep track of CUDA/HIP device-side variables ODR-used by host code.
1104  llvm::DenseSet<const VarDecl *> CUDADeviceVarODRUsedByHost;
1105 
1106  ASTContext(LangOptions &LOpts, SourceManager &SM, IdentifierTable &idents,
1107  SelectorTable &sels, Builtin::Context &builtins);
1108  ASTContext(const ASTContext &) = delete;
1109  ASTContext &operator=(const ASTContext &) = delete;
1110  ~ASTContext();
1111 
1112  /// Attach an external AST source to the AST context.
1113  ///
1114  /// The external AST source provides the ability to load parts of
1115  /// the abstract syntax tree as needed from some external storage,
1116  /// e.g., a precompiled header.
1117  void setExternalSource(IntrusiveRefCntPtr<ExternalASTSource> Source);
1118 
1119  /// Retrieve a pointer to the external AST source associated
1120  /// with this AST context, if any.
1121  ExternalASTSource *getExternalSource() const {
1122  return ExternalSource.get();
1123  }
1124 
1125  /// Attach an AST mutation listener to the AST context.
1126  ///
1127  /// The AST mutation listener provides the ability to track modifications to
1128  /// the abstract syntax tree entities committed after they were initially
1129  /// created.
1130  void setASTMutationListener(ASTMutationListener *Listener) {
1131  this->Listener = Listener;
1132  }
1133 
1134  /// Retrieve a pointer to the AST mutation listener associated
1135  /// with this AST context, if any.
1136  ASTMutationListener *getASTMutationListener() const { return Listener; }
1137 
1138  void PrintStats() const;
1139  const SmallVectorImpl<Type *>& getTypes() const { return Types; }
1140 
1141  BuiltinTemplateDecl *buildBuiltinTemplateDecl(BuiltinTemplateKind BTK,
1142  const IdentifierInfo *II) const;
1143 
1144  /// Create a new implicit TU-level CXXRecordDecl or RecordDecl
1145  /// declaration.
1146  RecordDecl *buildImplicitRecord(StringRef Name,
1147  RecordDecl::TagKind TK = TTK_Struct) const;
1148 
1149  /// Create a new implicit TU-level typedef declaration.
1150  TypedefDecl *buildImplicitTypedef(QualType T, StringRef Name) const;
1151 
1152  /// Retrieve the declaration for the 128-bit signed integer type.
1153  TypedefDecl *getInt128Decl() const;
1154 
1155  /// Retrieve the declaration for the 128-bit unsigned integer type.
1156  TypedefDecl *getUInt128Decl() const;
1157 
1158  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
1159  // Type Constructors
1160  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
1161 
1162 private:
1163  /// Return a type with extended qualifiers.
1164  QualType getExtQualType(const Type *Base, Qualifiers Quals) const;
1165 
1166  QualType getTypeDeclTypeSlow(const TypeDecl *Decl) const;
1167 
1168  QualType getPipeType(QualType T, bool ReadOnly) const;
1169 
1170 public:
1171  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for an address space
1172  /// qualified type with the specified type and address space.
1173  ///
1174  /// The resulting type has a union of the qualifiers from T and the address
1175  /// space. If T already has an address space specifier, it is silently
1176  /// replaced.
1177  QualType getAddrSpaceQualType(QualType T, LangAS AddressSpace) const;
1178 
1179  /// Remove any existing address space on the type and returns the type
1180  /// with qualifiers intact (or that's the idea anyway)
1181  ///
1182  /// The return type should be T with all prior qualifiers minus the address
1183  /// space.
1184  QualType removeAddrSpaceQualType(QualType T) const;
1185 
1186  /// Apply Objective-C protocol qualifiers to the given type.
1187  /// \param allowOnPointerType specifies if we can apply protocol
1188  /// qualifiers on ObjCObjectPointerType. It can be set to true when
1189  /// constructing the canonical type of a Objective-C type parameter.
1190  QualType applyObjCProtocolQualifiers(QualType type,
1191  ArrayRef<ObjCProtocolDecl *> protocols, bool &hasError,
1192  bool allowOnPointerType = false) const;
1193 
1194  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for an Objective-C
1195  /// gc-qualified type.
1196  ///
1197  /// The resulting type has a union of the qualifiers from T and the gc
1198  /// attribute.
1199  QualType getObjCGCQualType(QualType T, Qualifiers::GC gcAttr) const;
1200 
1201  /// Remove the existing address space on the type if it is a pointer size
1202  /// address space and return the type with qualifiers intact.
1203  QualType removePtrSizeAddrSpace(QualType T) const;
1204 
1205  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for a \c restrict
1206  /// qualified type.
1207  ///
1208  /// The resulting type has a union of the qualifiers from \p T and
1209  /// \c restrict.
1210  QualType getRestrictType(QualType T) const {
1211  return T.withFastQualifiers(Qualifiers::Restrict);
1212  }
1213 
1214  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for a \c volatile
1215  /// qualified type.
1216  ///
1217  /// The resulting type has a union of the qualifiers from \p T and
1218  /// \c volatile.
1219  QualType getVolatileType(QualType T) const {
1220  return T.withFastQualifiers(Qualifiers::Volatile);
1221  }
1222 
1223  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for a \c const
1224  /// qualified type.
1225  ///
1226  /// The resulting type has a union of the qualifiers from \p T and \c const.
1227  ///
1228  /// It can be reasonably expected that this will always be equivalent to
1229  /// calling T.withConst().
1230  QualType getConstType(QualType T) const { return T.withConst(); }
1231 
1232  /// Change the ExtInfo on a function type.
1233  const FunctionType *adjustFunctionType(const FunctionType *Fn,
1234  FunctionType::ExtInfo EInfo);
1235 
1236  /// Adjust the given function result type.
1237  CanQualType getCanonicalFunctionResultType(QualType ResultType) const;
1238 
1239  /// Change the result type of a function type once it is deduced.
1240  void adjustDeducedFunctionResultType(FunctionDecl *FD, QualType ResultType);
1241 
1242  /// Get a function type and produce the equivalent function type with the
1243  /// specified exception specification. Type sugar that can be present on a
1244  /// declaration of a function with an exception specification is permitted
1245  /// and preserved. Other type sugar (for instance, typedefs) is not.
1246  QualType getFunctionTypeWithExceptionSpec(
1247  QualType Orig, const FunctionProtoType::ExceptionSpecInfo &ESI);
1248 
1249  /// Determine whether two function types are the same, ignoring
1250  /// exception specifications in cases where they're part of the type.
1251  bool hasSameFunctionTypeIgnoringExceptionSpec(QualType T, QualType U);
1252 
1253  /// Change the exception specification on a function once it is
1254  /// delay-parsed, instantiated, or computed.
1255  void adjustExceptionSpec(FunctionDecl *FD,
1256  const FunctionProtoType::ExceptionSpecInfo &ESI,
1257  bool AsWritten = false);
1258 
1259  /// Get a function type and produce the equivalent function type where
1260  /// pointer size address spaces in the return type and parameter tyeps are
1261  /// replaced with the default address space.
1262  QualType getFunctionTypeWithoutPtrSizes(QualType T);
1263 
1264  /// Determine whether two function types are the same, ignoring pointer sizes
1265  /// in the return type and parameter types.
1266  bool hasSameFunctionTypeIgnoringPtrSizes(QualType T, QualType U);
1267 
1268  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for a complex
1269  /// number with the specified element type.
1270  QualType getComplexType(QualType T) const;
1271  CanQualType getComplexType(CanQualType T) const {
1272  return CanQualType::CreateUnsafe(getComplexType((QualType) T));
1273  }
1274 
1275  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for a pointer to
1276  /// the specified type.
1277  QualType getPointerType(QualType T) const;
1278  CanQualType getPointerType(CanQualType T) const {
1279  return CanQualType::CreateUnsafe(getPointerType((QualType) T));
1280  }
1281 
1282  /// Return the uniqued reference to a type adjusted from the original
1283  /// type to a new type.
1284  QualType getAdjustedType(QualType Orig, QualType New) const;
1285  CanQualType getAdjustedType(CanQualType Orig, CanQualType New) const {
1286  return CanQualType::CreateUnsafe(
1287  getAdjustedType((QualType)Orig, (QualType)New));
1288  }
1289 
1290  /// Return the uniqued reference to the decayed version of the given
1291  /// type. Can only be called on array and function types which decay to
1292  /// pointer types.
1293  QualType getDecayedType(QualType T) const;
1294  CanQualType getDecayedType(CanQualType T) const {
1295  return CanQualType::CreateUnsafe(getDecayedType((QualType) T));
1296  }
1297 
1298  /// Return the uniqued reference to the atomic type for the specified
1299  /// type.
1300  QualType getAtomicType(QualType T) const;
1301 
1302  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for a block of the
1303  /// specified type.
1304  QualType getBlockPointerType(QualType T) const;
1305 
1306  /// Gets the struct used to keep track of the descriptor for pointer to
1307  /// blocks.
1308  QualType getBlockDescriptorType() const;
1309 
1310  /// Return a read_only pipe type for the specified type.
1311  QualType getReadPipeType(QualType T) const;
1312 
1313  /// Return a write_only pipe type for the specified type.
1314  QualType getWritePipeType(QualType T) const;
1315 
1316  /// Return an extended integer type with the specified signedness and bit
1317  /// count.
1318  QualType getExtIntType(bool Unsigned, unsigned NumBits) const;
1319 
1320  /// Return a dependent extended integer type with the specified signedness and
1321  /// bit count.
1322  QualType getDependentExtIntType(bool Unsigned, Expr *BitsExpr) const;
1323 
1324  /// Gets the struct used to keep track of the extended descriptor for
1325  /// pointer to blocks.
1326  QualType getBlockDescriptorExtendedType() const;
1327 
1328  /// Map an AST Type to an OpenCLTypeKind enum value.
1329  OpenCLTypeKind getOpenCLTypeKind(const Type *T) const;
1330 
1331  /// Get address space for OpenCL type.
1332  LangAS getOpenCLTypeAddrSpace(const Type *T) const;
1333 
1334  void setcudaConfigureCallDecl(FunctionDecl *FD) {
1335  cudaConfigureCallDecl = FD;
1336  }
1337 
1338  FunctionDecl *getcudaConfigureCallDecl() {
1339  return cudaConfigureCallDecl;
1340  }
1341 
1342  /// Returns true iff we need copy/dispose helpers for the given type.
1343  bool BlockRequiresCopying(QualType Ty, const VarDecl *D);
1344 
1345  /// Returns true, if given type has a known lifetime. HasByrefExtendedLayout
1346  /// is set to false in this case. If HasByrefExtendedLayout returns true,
1347  /// byref variable has extended lifetime.
1348  bool getByrefLifetime(QualType Ty,
1349  Qualifiers::ObjCLifetime &Lifetime,
1350  bool &HasByrefExtendedLayout) const;
1351 
1352  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for an lvalue reference
1353  /// to the specified type.
1354  QualType getLValueReferenceType(QualType T, bool SpelledAsLValue = true)
1355  const;
1356 
1357  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for an rvalue reference
1358  /// to the specified type.
1359  QualType getRValueReferenceType(QualType T) const;
1360 
1361  /// Return the uniqued reference to the type for a member pointer to
1362  /// the specified type in the specified class.
1363  ///
1364  /// The class \p Cls is a \c Type because it could be a dependent name.
1365  QualType getMemberPointerType(QualType T, const Type *Cls) const;
1366 
1367  /// Return a non-unique reference to the type for a variable array of
1368  /// the specified element type.
1369  QualType getVariableArrayType(QualType EltTy, Expr *NumElts,
1370  ArrayType::ArraySizeModifier ASM,
1371  unsigned IndexTypeQuals,
1372  SourceRange Brackets) const;
1373 
1374  /// Return a non-unique reference to the type for a dependently-sized
1375  /// array of the specified element type.
1376  ///
1377  /// FIXME: We will need these to be uniqued, or at least comparable, at some
1378  /// point.
1379  QualType getDependentSizedArrayType(QualType EltTy, Expr *NumElts,
1380  ArrayType::ArraySizeModifier ASM,
1381  unsigned IndexTypeQuals,
1382  SourceRange Brackets) const;
1383 
1384  /// Return a unique reference to the type for an incomplete array of
1385  /// the specified element type.
1386  QualType getIncompleteArrayType(QualType EltTy,
1387  ArrayType::ArraySizeModifier ASM,
1388  unsigned IndexTypeQuals) const;
1389 
1390  /// Return the unique reference to the type for a constant array of
1391  /// the specified element type.
1392  QualType getConstantArrayType(QualType EltTy, const llvm::APInt &ArySize,
1393  const Expr *SizeExpr,
1394  ArrayType::ArraySizeModifier ASM,
1395  unsigned IndexTypeQuals) const;
1396 
1397  /// Return a type for a constant array for a string literal of the
1398  /// specified element type and length.
1399  QualType getStringLiteralArrayType(QualType EltTy, unsigned Length) const;
1400 
1401  /// Returns a vla type where known sizes are replaced with [*].
1402  QualType getVariableArrayDecayedType(QualType Ty) const;
1403 
1404  // Convenience struct to return information about a builtin vector type.
1405  struct BuiltinVectorTypeInfo {
1406  QualType ElementType;
1407  llvm::ElementCount EC;
1408  unsigned NumVectors;
1409  BuiltinVectorTypeInfo(QualType ElementType, llvm::ElementCount EC,
1410  unsigned NumVectors)
1411  : ElementType(ElementType), EC(EC), NumVectors(NumVectors) {}
1412  };
1413 
1414  /// Returns the element type, element count and number of vectors
1415  /// (in case of tuple) for a builtin vector type.
1416  BuiltinVectorTypeInfo
1417  getBuiltinVectorTypeInfo(const BuiltinType *VecTy) const;
1418 
1419  /// Return the unique reference to a scalable vector type of the specified
1420  /// element type and scalable number of elements.
1421  ///
1422  /// \pre \p EltTy must be a built-in type.
1423  QualType getScalableVectorType(QualType EltTy, unsigned NumElts) const;
1424 
1425  /// Return the unique reference to a vector type of the specified
1426  /// element type and size.
1427  ///
1428  /// \pre \p VectorType must be a built-in type.
1429  QualType getVectorType(QualType VectorType, unsigned NumElts,
1430  VectorType::VectorKind VecKind) const;
1431  /// Return the unique reference to the type for a dependently sized vector of
1432  /// the specified element type.
1433  QualType getDependentVectorType(QualType VectorType, Expr *SizeExpr,
1434  SourceLocation AttrLoc,
1435  VectorType::VectorKind VecKind) const;
1436 
1437  /// Return the unique reference to an extended vector type
1438  /// of the specified element type and size.
1439  ///
1440  /// \pre \p VectorType must be a built-in type.
1441  QualType getExtVectorType(QualType VectorType, unsigned NumElts) const;
1442 
1443  /// \pre Return a non-unique reference to the type for a dependently-sized
1444  /// vector of the specified element type.
1445  ///
1446  /// FIXME: We will need these to be uniqued, or at least comparable, at some
1447  /// point.
1448  QualType getDependentSizedExtVectorType(QualType VectorType,
1449  Expr *SizeExpr,
1450  SourceLocation AttrLoc) const;
1451 
1452  /// Return the unique reference to the matrix type of the specified element
1453  /// type and size
1454  ///
1455  /// \pre \p ElementType must be a valid matrix element type (see
1456  /// MatrixType::isValidElementType).
1457  QualType getConstantMatrixType(QualType ElementType, unsigned NumRows,
1458  unsigned NumColumns) const;
1459 
1460  /// Return the unique reference to the matrix type of the specified element
1461  /// type and size
1462  QualType getDependentSizedMatrixType(QualType ElementType, Expr *RowExpr,
1463  Expr *ColumnExpr,
1464  SourceLocation AttrLoc) const;
1465 
1466  QualType getDependentAddressSpaceType(QualType PointeeType,
1467  Expr *AddrSpaceExpr,
1468  SourceLocation AttrLoc) const;
1469 
1470  /// Return a K&R style C function type like 'int()'.
1471  QualType getFunctionNoProtoType(QualType ResultTy,
1472  const FunctionType::ExtInfo &Info) const;
1473 
1474  QualType getFunctionNoProtoType(QualType ResultTy) const {
1475  return getFunctionNoProtoType(ResultTy, FunctionType::ExtInfo());
1476  }
1477 
1478  /// Return a normal function type with a typed argument list.
1479  QualType getFunctionType(QualType ResultTy, ArrayRef<QualType> Args,
1480  const FunctionProtoType::ExtProtoInfo &EPI) const {
1481  return getFunctionTypeInternal(ResultTy, Args, EPI, false);
1482  }
1483 
1484  QualType adjustStringLiteralBaseType(QualType StrLTy) const;
1485 
1486 private:
1487  /// Return a normal function type with a typed argument list.
1488  QualType getFunctionTypeInternal(QualType ResultTy, ArrayRef<QualType> Args,
1489  const FunctionProtoType::ExtProtoInfo &EPI,
1490  bool OnlyWantCanonical) const;
1491 
1492 public:
1493  /// Return the unique reference to the type for the specified type
1494  /// declaration.
1495  QualType getTypeDeclType(const TypeDecl *Decl,
1496  const TypeDecl *PrevDecl = nullptr) const {
1497  assert(Decl && "Passed null for Decl param");
1498  if (Decl->TypeForDecl) return QualType(Decl->TypeForDecl, 0);
1499 
1500  if (PrevDecl) {
1501  assert(PrevDecl->TypeForDecl && "previous decl has no TypeForDecl");
1502  Decl->TypeForDecl = PrevDecl->TypeForDecl;
1503  return QualType(PrevDecl->TypeForDecl, 0);
1504  }
1505 
1506  return getTypeDeclTypeSlow(Decl);
1507  }
1508 
1509  /// Return the unique reference to the type for the specified
1510  /// typedef-name decl.
1511  QualType getTypedefType(const TypedefNameDecl *Decl,
1512  QualType Underlying = QualType()) const;
1513 
1514  QualType getRecordType(const RecordDecl *Decl) const;
1515 
1516  QualType getEnumType(const EnumDecl *Decl) const;
1517 
1518  QualType getInjectedClassNameType(CXXRecordDecl *Decl, QualType TST) const;
1519 
1520  QualType getAttributedType(attr::Kind attrKind,
1521  QualType modifiedType,
1522  QualType equivalentType);
1523 
1524  QualType getSubstTemplateTypeParmType(const TemplateTypeParmType *Replaced,
1525  QualType Replacement) const;
1526  QualType getSubstTemplateTypeParmPackType(
1527  const TemplateTypeParmType *Replaced,
1528  const TemplateArgument &ArgPack);
1529 
1530  QualType
1531  getTemplateTypeParmType(unsigned Depth, unsigned Index,
1532  bool ParameterPack,
1533  TemplateTypeParmDecl *ParmDecl = nullptr) const;
1534 
1535  QualType getTemplateSpecializationType(TemplateName T,
1536  ArrayRef<TemplateArgument> Args,
1537  QualType Canon = QualType()) const;
1538 
1539  QualType
1540  getCanonicalTemplateSpecializationType(TemplateName T,
1541  ArrayRef<TemplateArgument> Args) const;
1542 
1543  QualType getTemplateSpecializationType(TemplateName T,
1544  const TemplateArgumentListInfo &Args,
1545  QualType Canon = QualType()) const;
1546 
1547  TypeSourceInfo *
1548  getTemplateSpecializationTypeInfo(TemplateName T, SourceLocation TLoc,
1549  const TemplateArgumentListInfo &Args,
1550  QualType Canon = QualType()) const;
1551 
1552  QualType getParenType(QualType NamedType) const;
1553 
1554  QualType getMacroQualifiedType(QualType UnderlyingTy,
1555  const IdentifierInfo *MacroII) const;
1556 
1557  QualType getElaboratedType(ElaboratedTypeKeyword Keyword,
1558  NestedNameSpecifier *NNS, QualType NamedType,
1559  TagDecl *OwnedTagDecl = nullptr) const;
1560  QualType getDependentNameType(ElaboratedTypeKeyword Keyword,
1561  NestedNameSpecifier *NNS,
1562  const IdentifierInfo *Name,
1563  QualType Canon = QualType()) const;
1564 
1565  QualType getDependentTemplateSpecializationType(ElaboratedTypeKeyword Keyword,
1566  NestedNameSpecifier *NNS,
1567  const IdentifierInfo *Name,
1568  const TemplateArgumentListInfo &Args) const;
1569  QualType getDependentTemplateSpecializationType(
1570  ElaboratedTypeKeyword Keyword, NestedNameSpecifier *NNS,
1571  const IdentifierInfo *Name, ArrayRef<TemplateArgument> Args) const;
1572 
1573  TemplateArgument getInjectedTemplateArg(NamedDecl *ParamDecl);
1574 
1575  /// Get a template argument list with one argument per template parameter
1576  /// in a template parameter list, such as for the injected class name of
1577  /// a class template.
1578  void getInjectedTemplateArgs(const TemplateParameterList *Params,
1579  SmallVectorImpl<TemplateArgument> &Args);
1580 
1581  /// Form a pack expansion type with the given pattern.
1582  /// \param NumExpansions The number of expansions for the pack, if known.
1583  /// \param ExpectPackInType If \c false, we should not expect \p Pattern to
1584  /// contain an unexpanded pack. This only makes sense if the pack
1585  /// expansion is used in a context where the arity is inferred from
1586  /// elsewhere, such as if the pattern contains a placeholder type or
1587  /// if this is the canonical type of another pack expansion type.
1588  QualType getPackExpansionType(QualType Pattern,
1589  Optional<unsigned> NumExpansions,
1590  bool ExpectPackInType = true);
1591 
1592  QualType getObjCInterfaceType(const ObjCInterfaceDecl *Decl,
1593  ObjCInterfaceDecl *PrevDecl = nullptr) const;
1594 
1595  /// Legacy interface: cannot provide type arguments or __kindof.
1596  QualType getObjCObjectType(QualType Base,
1597  ObjCProtocolDecl * const *Protocols,
1598  unsigned NumProtocols) const;
1599 
1600  QualType getObjCObjectType(QualType Base,
1601  ArrayRef<QualType> typeArgs,
1602  ArrayRef<ObjCProtocolDecl *> protocols,
1603  bool isKindOf) const;
1604 
1605  QualType getObjCTypeParamType(const ObjCTypeParamDecl *Decl,
1606  ArrayRef<ObjCProtocolDecl *> protocols) const;
1607  void adjustObjCTypeParamBoundType(const ObjCTypeParamDecl *Orig,
1608  ObjCTypeParamDecl *New) const;
1609 
1610  bool ObjCObjectAdoptsQTypeProtocols(QualType QT, ObjCInterfaceDecl *Decl);
1611 
1612  /// QIdProtocolsAdoptObjCObjectProtocols - Checks that protocols in
1613  /// QT's qualified-id protocol list adopt all protocols in IDecl's list
1614  /// of protocols.
1615  bool QIdProtocolsAdoptObjCObjectProtocols(QualType QT,
1616  ObjCInterfaceDecl *IDecl);
1617 
1618  /// Return a ObjCObjectPointerType type for the given ObjCObjectType.
1619  QualType getObjCObjectPointerType(QualType OIT) const;
1620 
1621  /// GCC extension.
1622  QualType getTypeOfExprType(Expr *e) const;
1623  QualType getTypeOfType(QualType t) const;
1624 
1625  /// C++11 decltype.
1626  QualType getDecltypeType(Expr *e, QualType UnderlyingType) const;
1627 
1628  /// Unary type transforms
1629  QualType getUnaryTransformType(QualType BaseType, QualType UnderlyingType,
1630  UnaryTransformType::UTTKind UKind) const;
1631 
1632  /// C++11 deduced auto type.
1633  QualType getAutoType(QualType DeducedType, AutoTypeKeyword Keyword,
1634  bool IsDependent, bool IsPack = false,
1635  ConceptDecl *TypeConstraintConcept = nullptr,
1636  ArrayRef<TemplateArgument> TypeConstraintArgs ={}) const;
1637 
1638  /// C++11 deduction pattern for 'auto' type.
1639  QualType getAutoDeductType() const;
1640 
1641  /// C++11 deduction pattern for 'auto &&' type.
1642  QualType getAutoRRefDeductType() const;
1643 
1644  /// C++17 deduced class template specialization type.
1645  QualType getDeducedTemplateSpecializationType(TemplateName Template,
1646  QualType DeducedType,
1647  bool IsDependent) const;
1648 
1649  /// Return the unique reference to the type for the specified TagDecl
1650  /// (struct/union/class/enum) decl.
1651  QualType getTagDeclType(const TagDecl *Decl) const;
1652 
1653  /// Return the unique type for "size_t" (C99 7.17), defined in
1654  /// <stddef.h>.
1655  ///
1656  /// The sizeof operator requires this (C99 6.5.3.4p4).
1657  CanQualType getSizeType() const;
1658 
1659  /// Return the unique signed counterpart of
1660  /// the integer type corresponding to size_t.
1661  CanQualType getSignedSizeType() const;
1662 
1663  /// Return the unique type for "intmax_t" (C99 7.18.1.5), defined in
1664  /// <stdint.h>.
1665  CanQualType getIntMaxType() const;
1666 
1667  /// Return the unique type for "uintmax_t" (C99 7.18.1.5), defined in
1668  /// <stdint.h>.
1669  CanQualType getUIntMaxType() const;
1670 
1671  /// Return the unique wchar_t type available in C++ (and available as
1672  /// __wchar_t as a Microsoft extension).
1673  QualType getWCharType() const { return WCharTy; }
1674 
1675  /// Return the type of wide characters. In C++, this returns the
1676  /// unique wchar_t type. In C99, this returns a type compatible with the type
1677  /// defined in <stddef.h> as defined by the target.
1678  QualType getWideCharType() const { return WideCharTy; }
1679 
1680  /// Return the type of "signed wchar_t".
1681  ///
1682  /// Used when in C++, as a GCC extension.
1683  QualType getSignedWCharType() const;
1684 
1685  /// Return the type of "unsigned wchar_t".
1686  ///
1687  /// Used when in C++, as a GCC extension.
1688  QualType getUnsignedWCharType() const;
1689 
1690  /// In C99, this returns a type compatible with the type
1691  /// defined in <stddef.h> as defined by the target.
1692  QualType getWIntType() const { return WIntTy; }
1693 
1694  /// Return a type compatible with "intptr_t" (C99 7.18.1.4),
1695  /// as defined by the target.
1696  QualType getIntPtrType() const;
1697 
1698  /// Return a type compatible with "uintptr_t" (C99 7.18.1.4),
1699  /// as defined by the target.
1700  QualType getUIntPtrType() const;
1701 
1702  /// Return the unique type for "ptrdiff_t" (C99 7.17) defined in
1703  /// <stddef.h>. Pointer - pointer requires this (C99 6.5.6p9).
1704  QualType getPointerDiffType() const;
1705 
1706  /// Return the unique unsigned counterpart of "ptrdiff_t"
1707  /// integer type. The standard (C11 7.21.6.1p7) refers to this type
1708  /// in the definition of %tu format specifier.
1709  QualType getUnsignedPointerDiffType() const;
1710 
1711  /// Return the unique type for "pid_t" defined in
1712  /// <sys/types.h>. We need this to compute the correct type for vfork().
1713  QualType getProcessIDType() const;
1714 
1715  /// Return the C structure type used to represent constant CFStrings.
1716  QualType getCFConstantStringType() const;
1717 
1718  /// Returns the C struct type for objc_super
1719  QualType getObjCSuperType() const;
1720  void setObjCSuperType(QualType ST) { ObjCSuperType = ST; }
1721 
1722  /// Get the structure type used to representation CFStrings, or NULL
1723  /// if it hasn't yet been built.
1724  QualType getRawCFConstantStringType() const {
1725  if (CFConstantStringTypeDecl)
1726  return getTypedefType(CFConstantStringTypeDecl);
1727  return QualType();
1728  }
1729  void setCFConstantStringType(QualType T);
1730  TypedefDecl *getCFConstantStringDecl() const;
1731  RecordDecl *getCFConstantStringTagDecl() const;
1732 
1733  // This setter/getter represents the ObjC type for an NSConstantString.
1734  void setObjCConstantStringInterface(ObjCInterfaceDecl *Decl);
1735  QualType getObjCConstantStringInterface() const {
1736  return ObjCConstantStringType;
1737  }
1738 
1739  QualType getObjCNSStringType() const {
1740  return ObjCNSStringType;
1741  }
1742 
1743  void setObjCNSStringType(QualType T) {
1744  ObjCNSStringType = T;
1745  }
1746 
1747  /// Retrieve the type that \c id has been defined to, which may be
1748  /// different from the built-in \c id if \c id has been typedef'd.
1749  QualType getObjCIdRedefinitionType() const {
1750  if (ObjCIdRedefinitionType.isNull())
1751  return getObjCIdType();
1752  return ObjCIdRedefinitionType;
1753  }
1754 
1755  /// Set the user-written type that redefines \c id.
1756  void setObjCIdRedefinitionType(QualType RedefType) {
1757  ObjCIdRedefinitionType = RedefType;
1758  }
1759 
1760  /// Retrieve the type that \c Class has been defined to, which may be
1761  /// different from the built-in \c Class if \c Class has been typedef'd.
1762  QualType getObjCClassRedefinitionType() const {
1763  if (ObjCClassRedefinitionType.isNull())
1764  return getObjCClassType();
1765  return ObjCClassRedefinitionType;
1766  }
1767 
1768  /// Set the user-written type that redefines 'SEL'.
1769  void setObjCClassRedefinitionType(QualType RedefType) {
1770  ObjCClassRedefinitionType = RedefType;
1771  }
1772 
1773  /// Retrieve the type that 'SEL' has been defined to, which may be
1774  /// different from the built-in 'SEL' if 'SEL' has been typedef'd.
1775  QualType getObjCSelRedefinitionType() const {
1776  if (ObjCSelRedefinitionType.isNull())
1777  return getObjCSelType();
1778  return ObjCSelRedefinitionType;
1779  }
1780 
1781  /// Set the user-written type that redefines 'SEL'.
1782  void setObjCSelRedefinitionType(QualType RedefType) {
1783  ObjCSelRedefinitionType = RedefType;
1784  }
1785 
1786  /// Retrieve the identifier 'NSObject'.
1787  IdentifierInfo *getNSObjectName() const {
1788  if (!NSObjectName) {
1789  NSObjectName = &Idents.get("NSObject");
1790  }
1791 
1792  return NSObjectName;
1793  }
1794 
1795  /// Retrieve the identifier 'NSCopying'.
1796  IdentifierInfo *getNSCopyingName() {
1797  if (!NSCopyingName) {
1798  NSCopyingName = &Idents.get("NSCopying");
1799  }
1800 
1801  return NSCopyingName;
1802  }
1803 
1804  CanQualType getNSUIntegerType() const;
1805 
1806  CanQualType getNSIntegerType() const;
1807 
1808  /// Retrieve the identifier 'bool'.
1809  IdentifierInfo *getBoolName() const {
1810  if (!BoolName)
1811  BoolName = &Idents.get("bool");
1812  return BoolName;
1813  }
1814 
1815  IdentifierInfo *getMakeIntegerSeqName() const {
1816  if (!MakeIntegerSeqName)
1817  MakeIntegerSeqName = &Idents.get("__make_integer_seq");
1818  return MakeIntegerSeqName;
1819  }
1820 
1821  IdentifierInfo *getTypePackElementName() const {
1822  if (!TypePackElementName)
1823  TypePackElementName = &Idents.get("__type_pack_element");
1824  return TypePackElementName;
1825  }
1826 
1827  /// Retrieve the Objective-C "instancetype" type, if already known;
1828  /// otherwise, returns a NULL type;
1829  QualType getObjCInstanceType() {
1830  return getTypeDeclType(getObjCInstanceTypeDecl());
1831  }
1832 
1833  /// Retrieve the typedef declaration corresponding to the Objective-C
1834  /// "instancetype" type.
1835  TypedefDecl *getObjCInstanceTypeDecl();
1836 
1837  /// Set the type for the C FILE type.
1838  void setFILEDecl(TypeDecl *FILEDecl) { this->FILEDecl = FILEDecl; }
1839 
1840  /// Retrieve the C FILE type.
1841  QualType getFILEType() const {
1842  if (FILEDecl)
1843  return getTypeDeclType(FILEDecl);
1844  return QualType();
1845  }
1846 
1847  /// Set the type for the C jmp_buf type.
1848  void setjmp_bufDecl(TypeDecl *jmp_bufDecl) {
1849  this->jmp_bufDecl = jmp_bufDecl;
1850  }
1851 
1852  /// Retrieve the C jmp_buf type.
1853  QualType getjmp_bufType() const {
1854  if (jmp_bufDecl)
1855  return getTypeDeclType(jmp_bufDecl);
1856  return QualType();
1857  }
1858 
1859  /// Set the type for the C sigjmp_buf type.
1860  void setsigjmp_bufDecl(TypeDecl *sigjmp_bufDecl) {
1861  this->sigjmp_bufDecl = sigjmp_bufDecl;
1862  }
1863 
1864  /// Retrieve the C sigjmp_buf type.
1865  QualType getsigjmp_bufType() const {
1866  if (sigjmp_bufDecl)
1867  return getTypeDeclType(sigjmp_bufDecl);
1868  return QualType();
1869  }
1870 
1871  /// Set the type for the C ucontext_t type.
1872  void setucontext_tDecl(TypeDecl *ucontext_tDecl) {
1873  this->ucontext_tDecl = ucontext_tDecl;
1874  }
1875 
1876  /// Retrieve the C ucontext_t type.
1877  QualType getucontext_tType() const {
1878  if (ucontext_tDecl)
1879  return getTypeDeclType(ucontext_tDecl);
1880  return QualType();
1881  }
1882 
1883  /// The result type of logical operations, '<', '>', '!=', etc.
1884  QualType getLogicalOperationType() const {
1885  return getLangOpts().CPlusPlus ? BoolTy : IntTy;
1886  }
1887 
1888  /// Emit the Objective-CC type encoding for the given type \p T into
1889  /// \p S.
1890  ///
1891  /// If \p Field is specified then record field names are also encoded.
1892  void getObjCEncodingForType(QualType T, std::string &S,
1893  const FieldDecl *Field=nullptr,
1894  QualType *NotEncodedT=nullptr) const;
1895 
1896  /// Emit the Objective-C property type encoding for the given
1897  /// type \p T into \p S.
1898  void getObjCEncodingForPropertyType(QualType T, std::string &S) const;
1899 
1900  void getLegacyIntegralTypeEncoding(QualType &t) const;
1901 
1902  /// Put the string version of the type qualifiers \p QT into \p S.
1903  void getObjCEncodingForTypeQualifier(Decl::ObjCDeclQualifier QT,
1904  std::string &S) const;
1905 
1906  /// Emit the encoded type for the function \p Decl into \p S.
1907  ///
1908  /// This is in the same format as Objective-C method encodings.
1909  ///
1910  /// \returns true if an error occurred (e.g., because one of the parameter
1911  /// types is incomplete), false otherwise.
1912  std::string getObjCEncodingForFunctionDecl(const FunctionDecl *Decl) const;
1913 
1914  /// Emit the encoded type for the method declaration \p Decl into
1915  /// \p S.
1916  std::string getObjCEncodingForMethodDecl(const ObjCMethodDecl *Decl,
1917  bool Extended = false) const;
1918 
1919  /// Return the encoded type for this block declaration.
1920  std::string getObjCEncodingForBlock(const BlockExpr *blockExpr) const;
1921 
1922  /// getObjCEncodingForPropertyDecl - Return the encoded type for
1923  /// this method declaration. If non-NULL, Container must be either
1924  /// an ObjCCategoryImplDecl or ObjCImplementationDecl; it should
1925  /// only be NULL when getting encodings for protocol properties.
1926  std::string getObjCEncodingForPropertyDecl(const ObjCPropertyDecl *PD,
1927  const Decl *Container) const;
1928 
1929  bool ProtocolCompatibleWithProtocol(ObjCProtocolDecl *lProto,
1930  ObjCProtocolDecl *rProto) const;
1931 
1932  ObjCPropertyImplDecl *getObjCPropertyImplDeclForPropertyDecl(
1933  const ObjCPropertyDecl *PD,
1934  const Decl *Container) const;
1935 
1936  /// Return the size of type \p T for Objective-C encoding purpose,
1937  /// in characters.
1938  CharUnits getObjCEncodingTypeSize(QualType T) const;
1939 
1940  /// Retrieve the typedef corresponding to the predefined \c id type
1941  /// in Objective-C.
1942  TypedefDecl *getObjCIdDecl() const;
1943 
1944  /// Represents the Objective-CC \c id type.
1945  ///
1946  /// This is set up lazily, by Sema. \c id is always a (typedef for a)
1947  /// pointer type, a pointer to a struct.
1948  QualType getObjCIdType() const {
1949  return getTypeDeclType(getObjCIdDecl());
1950  }
1951 
1952  /// Retrieve the typedef corresponding to the predefined 'SEL' type
1953  /// in Objective-C.
1954  TypedefDecl *getObjCSelDecl() const;
1955 
1956  /// Retrieve the type that corresponds to the predefined Objective-C
1957  /// 'SEL' type.
1958  QualType getObjCSelType() const {
1959  return getTypeDeclType(getObjCSelDecl());
1960  }
1961 
1962  /// Retrieve the typedef declaration corresponding to the predefined
1963  /// Objective-C 'Class' type.
1964  TypedefDecl *getObjCClassDecl() const;
1965 
1966  /// Represents the Objective-C \c Class type.
1967  ///
1968  /// This is set up lazily, by Sema. \c Class is always a (typedef for a)
1969  /// pointer type, a pointer to a struct.
1970  QualType getObjCClassType() const {
1971  return getTypeDeclType(getObjCClassDecl());
1972  }
1973 
1974  /// Retrieve the Objective-C class declaration corresponding to
1975  /// the predefined \c Protocol class.
1976  ObjCInterfaceDecl *getObjCProtocolDecl() const;
1977 
1978  /// Retrieve declaration of 'BOOL' typedef
1979  TypedefDecl *getBOOLDecl() const {
1980  return BOOLDecl;
1981  }
1982 
1983  /// Save declaration of 'BOOL' typedef
1984  void setBOOLDecl(TypedefDecl *TD) {
1985  BOOLDecl = TD;
1986  }
1987 
1988  /// type of 'BOOL' type.
1989  QualType getBOOLType() const {
1990  return getTypeDeclType(getBOOLDecl());
1991  }
1992 
1993  /// Retrieve the type of the Objective-C \c Protocol class.
1994  QualType getObjCProtoType() const {
1995  return getObjCInterfaceType(getObjCProtocolDecl());
1996  }
1997 
1998  /// Retrieve the C type declaration corresponding to the predefined
1999  /// \c __builtin_va_list type.
2000  TypedefDecl *getBuiltinVaListDecl() const;
2001 
2002  /// Retrieve the type of the \c __builtin_va_list type.
2003  QualType getBuiltinVaListType() const {
2004  return getTypeDeclType(getBuiltinVaListDecl());
2005  }
2006 
2007  /// Retrieve the C type declaration corresponding to the predefined
2008  /// \c __va_list_tag type used to help define the \c __builtin_va_list type
2009  /// for some targets.
2010  Decl *getVaListTagDecl() const;
2011 
2012  /// Retrieve the C type declaration corresponding to the predefined
2013  /// \c __builtin_ms_va_list type.
2014  TypedefDecl *getBuiltinMSVaListDecl() const;
2015 
2016  /// Retrieve the type of the \c __builtin_ms_va_list type.
2017  QualType getBuiltinMSVaListType() const {
2018  return getTypeDeclType(getBuiltinMSVaListDecl());
2019  }
2020 
2021  /// Retrieve the implicitly-predeclared 'struct _GUID' declaration.
2022  TagDecl *getMSGuidTagDecl() const { return MSGuidTagDecl; }
2023 
2024  /// Retrieve the implicitly-predeclared 'struct _GUID' type.
2025  QualType getMSGuidType() const {
2026  assert(MSGuidTagDecl && "asked for GUID type but MS extensions disabled");
2027  return getTagDeclType(MSGuidTagDecl);
2028  }
2029 
2030  /// Return whether a declaration to a builtin is allowed to be
2031  /// overloaded/redeclared.
2032  bool canBuiltinBeRedeclared(const FunctionDecl *) const;
2033 
2034  /// Return a type with additional \c const, \c volatile, or
2035  /// \c restrict qualifiers.
2036  QualType getCVRQualifiedType(QualType T, unsigned CVR) const {
2037  return getQualifiedType(T, Qualifiers::fromCVRMask(CVR));
2038  }
2039 
2040  /// Un-split a SplitQualType.
2041  QualType getQualifiedType(SplitQualType split) const {
2042  return getQualifiedType(split.Ty, split.Quals);
2043  }
2044 
2045  /// Return a type with additional qualifiers.
2046  QualType getQualifiedType(QualType T, Qualifiers Qs) const {
2047  if (!Qs.hasNonFastQualifiers())
2048  return T.withFastQualifiers(Qs.getFastQualifiers());
2049  QualifierCollector Qc(Qs);
2050  const Type *Ptr = Qc.strip(T);
2051  return getExtQualType(Ptr, Qc);
2052  }
2053 
2054  /// Return a type with additional qualifiers.
2055  QualType getQualifiedType(const Type *T, Qualifiers Qs) const {
2056  if (!Qs.hasNonFastQualifiers())
2057  return QualType(T, Qs.getFastQualifiers());
2058  return getExtQualType(T, Qs);
2059  }
2060 
2061  /// Return a type with the given lifetime qualifier.
2062  ///
2063  /// \pre Neither type.ObjCLifetime() nor \p lifetime may be \c OCL_None.
2064  QualType getLifetimeQualifiedType(QualType type,
2065  Qualifiers::ObjCLifetime lifetime) {
2066  assert(type.getObjCLifetime() == Qualifiers::OCL_None);
2067  assert(lifetime != Qualifiers::OCL_None);
2068 
2069  Qualifiers qs;
2070  qs.addObjCLifetime(lifetime);
2071  return getQualifiedType(type, qs);
2072  }
2073 
2074  /// getUnqualifiedObjCPointerType - Returns version of
2075  /// Objective-C pointer type with lifetime qualifier removed.
2076  QualType getUnqualifiedObjCPointerType(QualType type) const {
2077  if (!type.getTypePtr()->isObjCObjectPointerType() ||
2078  !type.getQualifiers().hasObjCLifetime())
2079  return type;
2080  Qualifiers Qs = type.getQualifiers();
2081  Qs.removeObjCLifetime();
2082  return getQualifiedType(type.getUnqualifiedType(), Qs);
2083  }
2084 
2085  unsigned char getFixedPointScale(QualType Ty) const;
2086  unsigned char getFixedPointIBits(QualType Ty) const;
2087  llvm::FixedPointSemantics getFixedPointSemantics(QualType Ty) const;
2088  llvm::APFixedPoint getFixedPointMax(QualType Ty) const;
2089  llvm::APFixedPoint getFixedPointMin(QualType Ty) const;
2090 
2091  DeclarationNameInfo getNameForTemplate(TemplateName Name,
2092  SourceLocation NameLoc) const;
2093 
2094  TemplateName getOverloadedTemplateName(UnresolvedSetIterator Begin,
2095  UnresolvedSetIterator End) const;
2096  TemplateName getAssumedTemplateName(DeclarationName Name) const;
2097 
2098  TemplateName getQualifiedTemplateName(NestedNameSpecifier *NNS,
2099  bool TemplateKeyword,
2100  TemplateDecl *Template) const;
2101 
2102  TemplateName getDependentTemplateName(NestedNameSpecifier *NNS,
2103  const IdentifierInfo *Name) const;
2104  TemplateName getDependentTemplateName(NestedNameSpecifier *NNS,
2105  OverloadedOperatorKind Operator) const;
