2106  TemplateName getSubstTemplateTemplateParm(TemplateTemplateParmDecl *param,
2107  TemplateName replacement) const;
2108  TemplateName getSubstTemplateTemplateParmPack(TemplateTemplateParmDecl *Param,
2109  const TemplateArgument &ArgPack) const;
2110 
2111  enum GetBuiltinTypeError {
2112  /// No error
2113  GE_None,
2114 
2115  /// Missing a type
2116  GE_Missing_type,
2117 
2118  /// Missing a type from <stdio.h>
2119  GE_Missing_stdio,
2120 
2121  /// Missing a type from <setjmp.h>
2122  GE_Missing_setjmp,
2123 
2124  /// Missing a type from <ucontext.h>
2125  GE_Missing_ucontext
2126  };
2127 
2128  QualType DecodeTypeStr(const char *&Str, const ASTContext &Context,
2129  ASTContext::GetBuiltinTypeError &Error,
2130  bool &RequireICE, bool AllowTypeModifiers) const;
2131 
2132  /// Return the type for the specified builtin.
2133  ///
2134  /// If \p IntegerConstantArgs is non-null, it is filled in with a bitmask of
2135  /// arguments to the builtin that are required to be integer constant
2136  /// expressions.
2137  QualType GetBuiltinType(unsigned ID, GetBuiltinTypeError &Error,
2138  unsigned *IntegerConstantArgs = nullptr) const;
2139 
2140  /// Types and expressions required to build C++2a three-way comparisons
2141  /// using operator<=>, including the values return by builtin <=> operators.
2142  ComparisonCategories CompCategories;
2143 
2144 private:
2145  CanQualType getFromTargetType(unsigned Type) const;
2146  TypeInfo getTypeInfoImpl(const Type *T) const;
2147 
2148  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2149  // Type Predicates.
2150  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2151 
2152 public:
2153  /// Return one of the GCNone, Weak or Strong Objective-C garbage
2154  /// collection attributes.
2155  Qualifiers::GC getObjCGCAttrKind(QualType Ty) const;
2156 
2157  /// Return true if the given vector types are of the same unqualified
2158  /// type or if they are equivalent to the same GCC vector type.
2159  ///
2160  /// \note This ignores whether they are target-specific (AltiVec or Neon)
2161  /// types.
2162  bool areCompatibleVectorTypes(QualType FirstVec, QualType SecondVec);
2163 
2164  /// Return true if the given types are an SVE builtin and a VectorType that
2165  /// is a fixed-length representation of the SVE builtin for a specific
2166  /// vector-length.
2167  bool areCompatibleSveTypes(QualType FirstType, QualType SecondType);
2168 
2169  /// Return true if the given vector types are lax-compatible SVE vector types,
2170  /// false otherwise.
2171  bool areLaxCompatibleSveTypes(QualType FirstType, QualType SecondType);
2172 
2173  /// Return true if the type has been explicitly qualified with ObjC ownership.
2174  /// A type may be implicitly qualified with ownership under ObjC ARC, and in
2175  /// some cases the compiler treats these differently.
2176  bool hasDirectOwnershipQualifier(QualType Ty) const;
2177 
2178  /// Return true if this is an \c NSObject object with its \c NSObject
2179  /// attribute set.
2180  static bool isObjCNSObjectType(QualType Ty) {
2181  return Ty->isObjCNSObjectType();
2182  }
2183 
2184  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2185  // Type Sizing and Analysis
2186  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2187 
2188  /// Return the APFloat 'semantics' for the specified scalar floating
2189  /// point type.
2190  const llvm::fltSemantics &getFloatTypeSemantics(QualType T) const;
2191 
2192  /// Get the size and alignment of the specified complete type in bits.
2193  TypeInfo getTypeInfo(const Type *T) const;
2194  TypeInfo getTypeInfo(QualType T) const { return getTypeInfo(T.getTypePtr()); }
2195 
2196  /// Get default simd alignment of the specified complete type in bits.
2197  unsigned getOpenMPDefaultSimdAlign(QualType T) const;
2198 
2199  /// Return the size of the specified (complete) type \p T, in bits.
2200  uint64_t getTypeSize(QualType T) const { return getTypeInfo(T).Width; }
2201  uint64_t getTypeSize(const Type *T) const { return getTypeInfo(T).Width; }
2202 
2203  /// Return the size of the character type, in bits.
2204  uint64_t getCharWidth() const {
2205  return getTypeSize(CharTy);
2206  }
2207 
2208  /// Convert a size in bits to a size in characters.
2209  CharUnits toCharUnitsFromBits(int64_t BitSize) const;
2210 
2211  /// Convert a size in characters to a size in bits.
2212  int64_t toBits(CharUnits CharSize) const;
2213 
2214  /// Return the size of the specified (complete) type \p T, in
2215  /// characters.
2216  CharUnits getTypeSizeInChars(QualType T) const;
2217  CharUnits getTypeSizeInChars(const Type *T) const;
2218 
2219  Optional<CharUnits> getTypeSizeInCharsIfKnown(QualType Ty) const {
2220  if (Ty->isIncompleteType() || Ty->isDependentType())
2221  return None;
2222  return getTypeSizeInChars(Ty);
2223  }
2224 
2225  Optional<CharUnits> getTypeSizeInCharsIfKnown(const Type *Ty) const {
2226  return getTypeSizeInCharsIfKnown(QualType(Ty, 0));
2227  }
2228 
2229  /// Return the ABI-specified alignment of a (complete) type \p T, in
2230  /// bits.
2231  unsigned getTypeAlign(QualType T) const { return getTypeInfo(T).Align; }
2232  unsigned getTypeAlign(const Type *T) const { return getTypeInfo(T).Align; }
2233 
2234  /// Return the ABI-specified natural alignment of a (complete) type \p T,
2235  /// before alignment adjustments, in bits.
2236  ///
2237  /// This alignment is curently used only by ARM and AArch64 when passing
2238  /// arguments of a composite type.
2239  unsigned getTypeUnadjustedAlign(QualType T) const {
2240  return getTypeUnadjustedAlign(T.getTypePtr());
2241  }
2242  unsigned getTypeUnadjustedAlign(const Type *T) const;
2243 
2244  /// Return the alignment of a type, in bits, or 0 if
2245  /// the type is incomplete and we cannot determine the alignment (for
2246  /// example, from alignment attributes). The returned alignment is the
2247  /// Preferred alignment if NeedsPreferredAlignment is true, otherwise is the
2248  /// ABI alignment.
2249  unsigned getTypeAlignIfKnown(QualType T,
2250  bool NeedsPreferredAlignment = false) const;
2251 
2252  /// Return the ABI-specified alignment of a (complete) type \p T, in
2253  /// characters.
2254  CharUnits getTypeAlignInChars(QualType T) const;
2255  CharUnits getTypeAlignInChars(const Type *T) const;
2256 
2257  /// Return the PreferredAlignment of a (complete) type \p T, in
2258  /// characters.
2259  CharUnits getPreferredTypeAlignInChars(QualType T) const {
2260  return toCharUnitsFromBits(getPreferredTypeAlign(T));
2261  }
2262 
2263  /// getTypeUnadjustedAlignInChars - Return the ABI-specified alignment of a type,
2264  /// in characters, before alignment adjustments. This method does not work on
2265  /// incomplete types.
2266  CharUnits getTypeUnadjustedAlignInChars(QualType T) const;
2267  CharUnits getTypeUnadjustedAlignInChars(const Type *T) const;
2268 
2269  // getTypeInfoDataSizeInChars - Return the size of a type, in chars. If the
2270  // type is a record, its data size is returned.
2271  TypeInfoChars getTypeInfoDataSizeInChars(QualType T) const;
2272 
2273  TypeInfoChars getTypeInfoInChars(const Type *T) const;
2274  TypeInfoChars getTypeInfoInChars(QualType T) const;
2275 
2276  /// Determine if the alignment the type has was required using an
2277  /// alignment attribute.
2278  bool isAlignmentRequired(const Type *T) const;
2279  bool isAlignmentRequired(QualType T) const;
2280 
2281  /// Return the "preferred" alignment of the specified type \p T for
2282  /// the current target, in bits.
2283  ///
2284  /// This can be different than the ABI alignment in cases where it is
2285  /// beneficial for performance or backwards compatibility preserving to
2286  /// overalign a data type. (Note: despite the name, the preferred alignment
2287  /// is ABI-impacting, and not an optimization.)
2288  unsigned getPreferredTypeAlign(QualType T) const {
2289  return getPreferredTypeAlign(T.getTypePtr());
2290  }
2291  unsigned getPreferredTypeAlign(const Type *T) const;
2292 
2293  /// Return the default alignment for __attribute__((aligned)) on
2294  /// this target, to be used if no alignment value is specified.
2295  unsigned getTargetDefaultAlignForAttributeAligned() const;
2296 
2297  /// Return the alignment in bits that should be given to a
2298  /// global variable with type \p T.
2299  unsigned getAlignOfGlobalVar(QualType T) const;
2300 
2301  /// Return the alignment in characters that should be given to a
2302  /// global variable with type \p T.
2303  CharUnits getAlignOfGlobalVarInChars(QualType T) const;
2304 
2305  /// Return a conservative estimate of the alignment of the specified
2306  /// decl \p D.
2307  ///
2308  /// \pre \p D must not be a bitfield type, as bitfields do not have a valid
2309  /// alignment.
2310  ///
2311  /// If \p ForAlignof, references are treated like their underlying type
2312  /// and large arrays don't get any special treatment. If not \p ForAlignof
2313  /// it computes the value expected by CodeGen: references are treated like
2314  /// pointers and large arrays get extra alignment.
2315  CharUnits getDeclAlign(const Decl *D, bool ForAlignof = false) const;
2316 
2317  /// Return the alignment (in bytes) of the thrown exception object. This is
2318  /// only meaningful for targets that allocate C++ exceptions in a system
2319  /// runtime, such as those using the Itanium C++ ABI.
2320  CharUnits getExnObjectAlignment() const;
2321 
2322  /// Get or compute information about the layout of the specified
2323  /// record (struct/union/class) \p D, which indicates its size and field
2324  /// position information.
2325  const ASTRecordLayout &getASTRecordLayout(const RecordDecl *D) const;
2326 
2327  /// Get or compute information about the layout of the specified
2328  /// Objective-C interface.
2329  const ASTRecordLayout &getASTObjCInterfaceLayout(const ObjCInterfaceDecl *D)
2330  const;
2331 
2332  void DumpRecordLayout(const RecordDecl *RD, raw_ostream &OS,
2333  bool Simple = false) const;
2334 
2335  /// Get or compute information about the layout of the specified
2336  /// Objective-C implementation.
2337  ///
2338  /// This may differ from the interface if synthesized ivars are present.
2339  const ASTRecordLayout &
2340  getASTObjCImplementationLayout(const ObjCImplementationDecl *D) const;
2341 
2342  /// Get our current best idea for the key function of the
2343  /// given record decl, or nullptr if there isn't one.
2344  ///
2345  /// The key function is, according to the Itanium C++ ABI section 5.2.3:
2346  /// ...the first non-pure virtual function that is not inline at the
2347  /// point of class definition.
2348  ///
2349  /// Other ABIs use the same idea. However, the ARM C++ ABI ignores
2350  /// virtual functions that are defined 'inline', which means that
2351  /// the result of this computation can change.
2352  const CXXMethodDecl *getCurrentKeyFunction(const CXXRecordDecl *RD);
2353 
2354  /// Observe that the given method cannot be a key function.
2355  /// Checks the key-function cache for the method's class and clears it
2356  /// if matches the given declaration.
2357  ///
2358  /// This is used in ABIs where out-of-line definitions marked
2359  /// inline are not considered to be key functions.
2360  ///
2361  /// \param method should be the declaration from the class definition
2362  void setNonKeyFunction(const CXXMethodDecl *method);
2363 
2364  /// Loading virtual member pointers using the virtual inheritance model
2365  /// always results in an adjustment using the vbtable even if the index is
2366  /// zero.
2367  ///
2368  /// This is usually OK because the first slot in the vbtable points
2369  /// backwards to the top of the MDC. However, the MDC might be reusing a
2370  /// vbptr from an nv-base. In this case, the first slot in the vbtable
2371  /// points to the start of the nv-base which introduced the vbptr and *not*
2372  /// the MDC. Modify the NonVirtualBaseAdjustment to account for this.
2373  CharUnits getOffsetOfBaseWithVBPtr(const CXXRecordDecl *RD) const;
2374 
2375  /// Get the offset of a FieldDecl or IndirectFieldDecl, in bits.
2376  uint64_t getFieldOffset(const ValueDecl *FD) const;
2377 
2378  /// Get the offset of an ObjCIvarDecl in bits.
2379  uint64_t lookupFieldBitOffset(const ObjCInterfaceDecl *OID,
2380  const ObjCImplementationDecl *ID,
2381  const ObjCIvarDecl *Ivar) const;
2382 
2383  /// Find the 'this' offset for the member path in a pointer-to-member
2384  /// APValue.
2385  CharUnits getMemberPointerPathAdjustment(const APValue &MP) const;
2386 
2387  bool isNearlyEmpty(const CXXRecordDecl *RD) const;
2388 
2389  VTableContextBase *getVTableContext();
2390 
2391  /// If \p T is null pointer, assume the target in ASTContext.
2392  MangleContext *createMangleContext(const TargetInfo *T = nullptr);
2393 
2394  /// Creates a device mangle context to correctly mangle lambdas in a mixed
2395  /// architecture compile by setting the lambda mangling number source to the
2396  /// DeviceLambdaManglingNumber. Currently this asserts that the TargetInfo
2397  /// (from the AuxTargetInfo) is a an itanium target.
2398  MangleContext *createDeviceMangleContext(const TargetInfo &T);
2399 
2400  void DeepCollectObjCIvars(const ObjCInterfaceDecl *OI, bool leafClass,
2401  SmallVectorImpl<const ObjCIvarDecl*> &Ivars) const;
2402 
2403  unsigned CountNonClassIvars(const ObjCInterfaceDecl *OI) const;
2404  void CollectInheritedProtocols(const Decl *CDecl,
2405  llvm::SmallPtrSet<ObjCProtocolDecl*, 8> &Protocols);
2406 
2407  /// Return true if the specified type has unique object representations
2408  /// according to (C++17 [meta.unary.prop]p9)
2409  bool hasUniqueObjectRepresentations(QualType Ty) const;
2410 
2411  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2412  // Type Operators
2413  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2414 
2415  /// Return the canonical (structural) type corresponding to the
2416  /// specified potentially non-canonical type \p T.
2417  ///
2418  /// The non-canonical version of a type may have many "decorated" versions of
2419  /// types. Decorators can include typedefs, 'typeof' operators, etc. The
2420  /// returned type is guaranteed to be free of any of these, allowing two
2421  /// canonical types to be compared for exact equality with a simple pointer
2422  /// comparison.
2423  CanQualType getCanonicalType(QualType T) const {
2424  return CanQualType::CreateUnsafe(T.getCanonicalType());
2425  }
2426 
2427  const Type *getCanonicalType(const Type *T) const {
2428  return T->getCanonicalTypeInternal().getTypePtr();
2429  }
2430 
2431  /// Return the canonical parameter type corresponding to the specific
2432  /// potentially non-canonical one.
2433  ///
2434  /// Qualifiers are stripped off, functions are turned into function
2435  /// pointers, and arrays decay one level into pointers.
2436  CanQualType getCanonicalParamType(QualType T) const;
2437 
2438  /// Determine whether the given types \p T1 and \p T2 are equivalent.
2439  bool hasSameType(QualType T1, QualType T2) const {
2440  return getCanonicalType(T1) == getCanonicalType(T2);
2441  }
2442  bool hasSameType(const Type *T1, const Type *T2) const {
2443  return getCanonicalType(T1) == getCanonicalType(T2);
2444  }
2445 
2446  /// Return this type as a completely-unqualified array type,
2447  /// capturing the qualifiers in \p Quals.
2448  ///
2449  /// This will remove the minimal amount of sugaring from the types, similar
2450  /// to the behavior of QualType::getUnqualifiedType().
2451  ///
2452  /// \param T is the qualified type, which may be an ArrayType
2453  ///
2454  /// \param Quals will receive the full set of qualifiers that were
2455  /// applied to the array.
2456  ///
2457  /// \returns if this is an array type, the completely unqualified array type
2458  /// that corresponds to it. Otherwise, returns T.getUnqualifiedType().
2459  QualType getUnqualifiedArrayType(QualType T, Qualifiers &Quals);
2460 
2461  /// Determine whether the given types are equivalent after
2462  /// cvr-qualifiers have been removed.
2463  bool hasSameUnqualifiedType(QualType T1, QualType T2) const {
2464  return getCanonicalType(T1).getTypePtr() ==
2465  getCanonicalType(T2).getTypePtr();
2466  }
2467 
2468  bool hasSameNullabilityTypeQualifier(QualType SubT, QualType SuperT,
2469  bool IsParam) const {
2470  auto SubTnullability = SubT->getNullability(*this);
2471  auto SuperTnullability = SuperT->getNullability(*this);
2472  if (SubTnullability.hasValue() == SuperTnullability.hasValue()) {
2473  // Neither has nullability; return true
2474  if (!SubTnullability)
2475  return true;
2476  // Both have nullability qualifier.
2477  if (*SubTnullability == *SuperTnullability ||
2478  *SubTnullability == NullabilityKind::Unspecified ||
2479  *SuperTnullability == NullabilityKind::Unspecified)
2480  return true;
2481 
2482  if (IsParam) {
2483  // Ok for the superclass method parameter to be "nonnull" and the subclass
2484  // method parameter to be "nullable"
2485  return (*SuperTnullability == NullabilityKind::NonNull &&
2486  *SubTnullability == NullabilityKind::Nullable);
2487  }
2488  // For the return type, it's okay for the superclass method to specify
2489  // "nullable" and the subclass method specify "nonnull"
2490  return (*SuperTnullability == NullabilityKind::Nullable &&
2491  *SubTnullability == NullabilityKind::NonNull);
2492  }
2493  return true;
2494  }
2495 
2496  bool ObjCMethodsAreEqual(const ObjCMethodDecl *MethodDecl,
2497  const ObjCMethodDecl *MethodImp);
2498 
2499  bool UnwrapSimilarTypes(QualType &T1, QualType &T2);
2500  void UnwrapSimilarArrayTypes(QualType &T1, QualType &T2);
2501 
2502  /// Determine if two types are similar, according to the C++ rules. That is,
2503  /// determine if they are the same other than qualifiers on the initial
2504  /// sequence of pointer / pointer-to-member / array (and in Clang, object
2505  /// pointer) types and their element types.
2506  ///
2507  /// Clang offers a number of qualifiers in addition to the C++ qualifiers;
2508  /// those qualifiers are also ignored in the 'similarity' check.
2509  bool hasSimilarType(QualType T1, QualType T2);
2510 
2511  /// Determine if two types are similar, ignoring only CVR qualifiers.
2512  bool hasCvrSimilarType(QualType T1, QualType T2);
2513 
2514  /// Retrieves the "canonical" nested name specifier for a
2515  /// given nested name specifier.
2516  ///
2517  /// The canonical nested name specifier is a nested name specifier
2518  /// that uniquely identifies a type or namespace within the type
2519  /// system. For example, given:
2520  ///
2521  /// \code
2522  /// namespace N {
2523  /// struct S {
2524  /// template<typename T> struct X { typename T* type; };
2525  /// };
2526  /// }
2527  ///
2528  /// template<typename T> struct Y {
2529  /// typename N::S::X<T>::type member;
2530  /// };
2531  /// \endcode
2532  ///
2533  /// Here, the nested-name-specifier for N::S::X<T>:: will be
2534  /// S::X<template-param-0-0>, since 'S' and 'X' are uniquely defined
2535  /// by declarations in the type system and the canonical type for
2536  /// the template type parameter 'T' is template-param-0-0.
2537  NestedNameSpecifier *
2538  getCanonicalNestedNameSpecifier(NestedNameSpecifier *NNS) const;
2539 
2540  /// Retrieves the default calling convention for the current target.
2541  CallingConv getDefaultCallingConvention(bool IsVariadic,
2542  bool IsCXXMethod,
2543  bool IsBuiltin = false) const;
2544 
2545  /// Retrieves the "canonical" template name that refers to a
2546  /// given template.
2547  ///
2548  /// The canonical template name is the simplest expression that can
2549  /// be used to refer to a given template. For most templates, this
2550  /// expression is just the template declaration itself. For example,
2551  /// the template std::vector can be referred to via a variety of
2552  /// names---std::vector, \::std::vector, vector (if vector is in
2553  /// scope), etc.---but all of these names map down to the same
2554  /// TemplateDecl, which is used to form the canonical template name.
2555  ///
2556  /// Dependent template names are more interesting. Here, the
2557  /// template name could be something like T::template apply or
2558  /// std::allocator<T>::template rebind, where the nested name
2559  /// specifier itself is dependent. In this case, the canonical
2560  /// template name uses the shortest form of the dependent
2561  /// nested-name-specifier, which itself contains all canonical
2562  /// types, values, and templates.
2563  TemplateName getCanonicalTemplateName(TemplateName Name) const;
2564 
2565  /// Determine whether the given template names refer to the same
2566  /// template.
2567  bool hasSameTemplateName(TemplateName X, TemplateName Y);
2568 
2569  /// Retrieve the "canonical" template argument.
2570  ///
2571  /// The canonical template argument is the simplest template argument
2572  /// (which may be a type, value, expression, or declaration) that
2573  /// expresses the value of the argument.
2574  TemplateArgument getCanonicalTemplateArgument(const TemplateArgument &Arg)
2575  const;
2576 
2577  /// Type Query functions. If the type is an instance of the specified class,
2578  /// return the Type pointer for the underlying maximally pretty type. This
2579  /// is a member of ASTContext because this may need to do some amount of
2580  /// canonicalization, e.g. to move type qualifiers into the element type.
2581  const ArrayType *getAsArrayType(QualType T) const;
2582  const ConstantArrayType *getAsConstantArrayType(QualType T) const {
2583  return dyn_cast_or_null<ConstantArrayType>(getAsArrayType(T));
2584  }
2585  const VariableArrayType *getAsVariableArrayType(QualType T) const {
2586  return dyn_cast_or_null<VariableArrayType>(getAsArrayType(T));
2587  }
2588  const IncompleteArrayType *getAsIncompleteArrayType(QualType T) const {
2589  return dyn_cast_or_null<IncompleteArrayType>(getAsArrayType(T));
2590  }
2591  const DependentSizedArrayType *getAsDependentSizedArrayType(QualType T)
2592  const {
2593  return dyn_cast_or_null<DependentSizedArrayType>(getAsArrayType(T));
2594  }
2595 
2596  /// Return the innermost element type of an array type.
2597  ///
2598  /// For example, will return "int" for int[m][n]
2599  QualType getBaseElementType(const ArrayType *VAT) const;
2600 
2601  /// Return the innermost element type of a type (which needn't
2602  /// actually be an array type).
2603  QualType getBaseElementType(QualType QT) const;
2604 
2605  /// Return number of constant array elements.
2606  uint64_t getConstantArrayElementCount(const ConstantArrayType *CA) const;
2607 
2608  /// Perform adjustment on the parameter type of a function.
2609  ///
2610  /// This routine adjusts the given parameter type @p T to the actual
2611  /// parameter type used by semantic analysis (C99 6.7.5.3p[7,8],
2612  /// C++ [dcl.fct]p3). The adjusted parameter type is returned.
2613  QualType getAdjustedParameterType(QualType T) const;
2614 
2615  /// Retrieve the parameter type as adjusted for use in the signature
2616  /// of a function, decaying array and function types and removing top-level
2617  /// cv-qualifiers.
2618  QualType getSignatureParameterType(QualType T) const;
2619 
2620  QualType getExceptionObjectType(QualType T) const;
2621 
2622  /// Return the properly qualified result of decaying the specified
2623  /// array type to a pointer.
2624  ///
2625  /// This operation is non-trivial when handling typedefs etc. The canonical
2626  /// type of \p T must be an array type, this returns a pointer to a properly
2627  /// qualified element of the array.
2628  ///
2629  /// See C99 6.7.5.3p7 and C99 6.3.2.1p3.
2630  QualType getArrayDecayedType(QualType T) const;
2631 
2632  /// Return the type that \p PromotableType will promote to: C99
2633  /// 6.3.1.1p2, assuming that \p PromotableType is a promotable integer type.
2634  QualType getPromotedIntegerType(QualType PromotableType) const;
2635 
2636  /// Recurses in pointer/array types until it finds an Objective-C
2637  /// retainable type and returns its ownership.
2638  Qualifiers::ObjCLifetime getInnerObjCOwnership(QualType T) const;
2639 
2640  /// Whether this is a promotable bitfield reference according
2641  /// to C99 6.3.1.1p2, bullet 2 (and GCC extensions).
2642  ///
2643  /// \returns the type this bit-field will promote to, or NULL if no
2644  /// promotion occurs.
2645  QualType isPromotableBitField(Expr *E) const;
2646 
2647  /// Return the highest ranked integer type, see C99 6.3.1.8p1.
2648  ///
2649  /// If \p LHS > \p RHS, returns 1. If \p LHS == \p RHS, returns 0. If
2650  /// \p LHS < \p RHS, return -1.
2651  int getIntegerTypeOrder(QualType LHS, QualType RHS) const;
2652 
2653  /// Compare the rank of the two specified floating point types,
2654  /// ignoring the domain of the type (i.e. 'double' == '_Complex double').
2655  ///
2656  /// If \p LHS > \p RHS, returns 1. If \p LHS == \p RHS, returns 0. If
2657  /// \p LHS < \p RHS, return -1.
2658  int getFloatingTypeOrder(QualType LHS, QualType RHS) const;
2659 
2660  /// Compare the rank of two floating point types as above, but compare equal
2661  /// if both types have the same floating-point semantics on the target (i.e.
2662  /// long double and double on AArch64 will return 0).
2663  int getFloatingTypeSemanticOrder(QualType LHS, QualType RHS) const;
2664 
2665  /// Return a real floating point or a complex type (based on
2666  /// \p typeDomain/\p typeSize).
2667  ///
2668  /// \param typeDomain a real floating point or complex type.
2669  /// \param typeSize a real floating point or complex type.
2670  QualType getFloatingTypeOfSizeWithinDomain(QualType typeSize,
2671  QualType typeDomain) const;
2672 
2673  unsigned getTargetAddressSpace(QualType T) const {
2674  return getTargetAddressSpace(T.getQualifiers());
2675  }
2676 
2677  unsigned getTargetAddressSpace(Qualifiers Q) const {
2678  return getTargetAddressSpace(Q.getAddressSpace());
2679  }
2680 
2681  unsigned getTargetAddressSpace(LangAS AS) const;
2682 
2683  LangAS getLangASForBuiltinAddressSpace(unsigned AS) const;
2684 
2685  /// Get target-dependent integer value for null pointer which is used for
2686  /// constant folding.
2687  uint64_t getTargetNullPointerValue(QualType QT) const;
2688 
2689  bool addressSpaceMapManglingFor(LangAS AS) const {
2690  return AddrSpaceMapMangling || isTargetAddressSpace(AS);
2691  }
2692 
2693 private:
2694  // Helper for integer ordering
2695  unsigned getIntegerRank(const Type *T) const;
2696 
2697 public:
2698  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2699  // Type Compatibility Predicates
2700  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2701 
2702  /// Compatibility predicates used to check assignment expressions.
2703  bool typesAreCompatible(QualType T1, QualType T2,
2704  bool CompareUnqualified = false); // C99 6.2.7p1
2705 
2706  bool propertyTypesAreCompatible(QualType, QualType);
2707  bool typesAreBlockPointerCompatible(QualType, QualType);
2708 
2709  bool isObjCIdType(QualType T) const {
2710  return T == getObjCIdType();
2711  }
2712 
2713  bool isObjCClassType(QualType T) const {
2714  return T == getObjCClassType();
2715  }
2716 
2717  bool isObjCSelType(QualType T) const {
2718  return T == getObjCSelType();
2719  }
2720 
2721  bool ObjCQualifiedIdTypesAreCompatible(const ObjCObjectPointerType *LHS,
2722  const ObjCObjectPointerType *RHS,
2723  bool ForCompare);
2724 
2725  bool ObjCQualifiedClassTypesAreCompatible(const ObjCObjectPointerType *LHS,
2726  const ObjCObjectPointerType *RHS);
2727 
2728  // Check the safety of assignment from LHS to RHS
2729  bool canAssignObjCInterfaces(const ObjCObjectPointerType *LHSOPT,
2730  const ObjCObjectPointerType *RHSOPT);
2731  bool canAssignObjCInterfaces(const ObjCObjectType *LHS,
2732  const ObjCObjectType *RHS);
2733  bool canAssignObjCInterfacesInBlockPointer(
2734  const ObjCObjectPointerType *LHSOPT,
2735  const ObjCObjectPointerType *RHSOPT,
2736  bool BlockReturnType);
2737  bool areComparableObjCPointerTypes(QualType LHS, QualType RHS);
2738  QualType areCommonBaseCompatible(const ObjCObjectPointerType *LHSOPT,
2739  const ObjCObjectPointerType *RHSOPT);
2740  bool canBindObjCObjectType(QualType To, QualType From);
2741 
2742  // Functions for calculating composite types
2743  QualType mergeTypes(QualType, QualType, bool OfBlockPointer=false,
2744  bool Unqualified = false, bool BlockReturnType = false);
2745  QualType mergeFunctionTypes(QualType, QualType, bool OfBlockPointer=false,
2746  bool Unqualified = false, bool AllowCXX = false);
2747  QualType mergeFunctionParameterTypes(QualType, QualType,
2748  bool OfBlockPointer = false,
2749  bool Unqualified = false);
2750  QualType mergeTransparentUnionType(QualType, QualType,
2751  bool OfBlockPointer=false,
2752  bool Unqualified = false);
2753 
2754  QualType mergeObjCGCQualifiers(QualType, QualType);
2755 
2756  /// This function merges the ExtParameterInfo lists of two functions. It
2757  /// returns true if the lists are compatible. The merged list is returned in
2758  /// NewParamInfos.
2759  ///
2760  /// \param FirstFnType The type of the first function.
2761  ///
2762  /// \param SecondFnType The type of the second function.
2763  ///
2764  /// \param CanUseFirst This flag is set to true if the first function's
2765  /// ExtParameterInfo list can be used as the composite list of
2766  /// ExtParameterInfo.
2767  ///
2768  /// \param CanUseSecond This flag is set to true if the second function's
2769  /// ExtParameterInfo list can be used as the composite list of
2770  /// ExtParameterInfo.
2771  ///
2772  /// \param NewParamInfos The composite list of ExtParameterInfo. The list is
2773  /// empty if none of the flags are set.
2774  ///
2775  bool mergeExtParameterInfo(
2776  const FunctionProtoType *FirstFnType,
2777  const FunctionProtoType *SecondFnType,
2778  bool &CanUseFirst, bool &CanUseSecond,
2779  SmallVectorImpl<FunctionProtoType::ExtParameterInfo> &NewParamInfos);
2780 
2781  void ResetObjCLayout(const ObjCContainerDecl *CD);
2782 
2783  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2784  // Integer Predicates
2785  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2786 
2787  // The width of an integer, as defined in C99 6.2.6.2. This is the number
2788  // of bits in an integer type excluding any padding bits.
2789  unsigned getIntWidth(QualType T) const;
2790 
2791  // Per C99 6.2.5p6, for every signed integer type, there is a corresponding
2792  // unsigned integer type. This method takes a signed type, and returns the
2793  // corresponding unsigned integer type.
2794  // With the introduction of fixed point types in ISO N1169, this method also
2795  // accepts fixed point types and returns the corresponding unsigned type for
2796  // a given fixed point type.
2797  QualType getCorrespondingUnsignedType(QualType T) const;
2798 
2799  // Per C99 6.2.5p6, for every signed integer type, there is a corresponding
2800  // unsigned integer type. This method takes an unsigned type, and returns the
2801  // corresponding signed integer type.
2802  // With the introduction of fixed point types in ISO N1169, this method also
2803  // accepts fixed point types and returns the corresponding signed type for
2804  // a given fixed point type.
2805  QualType getCorrespondingSignedType(QualType T) const;
2806 
2807  // Per ISO N1169, this method accepts fixed point types and returns the
2808  // corresponding saturated type for a given fixed point type.
2809  QualType getCorrespondingSaturatedType(QualType Ty) const;
2810 
2811  // This method accepts fixed point types and returns the corresponding signed
2812  // type. Unlike getCorrespondingUnsignedType(), this only accepts unsigned
2813  // fixed point types because there are unsigned integer types like bool and
2814  // char8_t that don't have signed equivalents.
2815  QualType getCorrespondingSignedFixedPointType(QualType Ty) const;
2816 
2817  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2818  // Integer Values
2819  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2820 
2821  /// Make an APSInt of the appropriate width and signedness for the
2822  /// given \p Value and integer \p Type.
2823  llvm::APSInt MakeIntValue(uint64_t Value, QualType Type) const {
2824  // If Type is a signed integer type larger than 64 bits, we need to be sure
2825  // to sign extend Res appropriately.
2826  llvm::APSInt Res(64, !Type->isSignedIntegerOrEnumerationType());
2827  Res = Value;
2828  unsigned Width = getIntWidth(Type);
2829  if (Width != Res.getBitWidth())
2830  return Res.extOrTrunc(Width);
2831  return Res;
2832  }
2833 
2834  bool isSentinelNullExpr(const Expr *E);
2835 
2836  /// Get the implementation of the ObjCInterfaceDecl \p D, or nullptr if
2837  /// none exists.
2838  ObjCImplementationDecl *getObjCImplementation(ObjCInterfaceDecl *D);
2839 
2840  /// Get the implementation of the ObjCCategoryDecl \p D, or nullptr if
2841  /// none exists.
2842  ObjCCategoryImplDecl *getObjCImplementation(ObjCCategoryDecl *D);
2843 
2844  /// Return true if there is at least one \@implementation in the TU.
2845  bool AnyObjCImplementation() {
2846  return !ObjCImpls.empty();
2847  }
2848 
2849  /// Set the implementation of ObjCInterfaceDecl.
2850  void setObjCImplementation(ObjCInterfaceDecl *IFaceD,
2851  ObjCImplementationDecl *ImplD);
2852 
2853  /// Set the implementation of ObjCCategoryDecl.
2854  void setObjCImplementation(ObjCCategoryDecl *CatD,
2855  ObjCCategoryImplDecl *ImplD);
2856 
2857  /// Get the duplicate declaration of a ObjCMethod in the same
2858  /// interface, or null if none exists.
2859  const ObjCMethodDecl *
2860  getObjCMethodRedeclaration(const ObjCMethodDecl *MD) const;
2861 
2862  void setObjCMethodRedeclaration(const ObjCMethodDecl *MD,
2863  const ObjCMethodDecl *Redecl);
2864 
2865  /// Returns the Objective-C interface that \p ND belongs to if it is
2866  /// an Objective-C method/property/ivar etc. that is part of an interface,
2867  /// otherwise returns null.
2868  const ObjCInterfaceDecl *getObjContainingInterface(const NamedDecl *ND) const;
2869 
2870  /// Set the copy initialization expression of a block var decl. \p CanThrow
2871  /// indicates whether the copy expression can throw or not.
2872  void setBlockVarCopyInit(const VarDecl* VD, Expr *CopyExpr, bool CanThrow);
2873 
2874  /// Get the copy initialization expression of the VarDecl \p VD, or
2875  /// nullptr if none exists.
2876  BlockVarCopyInit getBlockVarCopyInit(const VarDecl* VD) const;
2877 
2878  /// Allocate an uninitialized TypeSourceInfo.
2879  ///
2880  /// The caller should initialize the memory held by TypeSourceInfo using
2881  /// the TypeLoc wrappers.
2882  ///
2883  /// \param T the type that will be the basis for type source info. This type
2884  /// should refer to how the declarator was written in source code, not to
2885  /// what type semantic analysis resolved the declarator to.
2886  ///
2887  /// \param Size the size of the type info to create, or 0 if the size
2888  /// should be calculated based on the type.
2889  TypeSourceInfo *CreateTypeSourceInfo(QualType T, unsigned Size = 0) const;
2890 
2891  /// Allocate a TypeSourceInfo where all locations have been
2892  /// initialized to a given location, which defaults to the empty
2893  /// location.
2894  TypeSourceInfo *
2895  getTrivialTypeSourceInfo(QualType T,
2896  SourceLocation Loc = SourceLocation()) const;
2897 
2898  /// Add a deallocation callback that will be invoked when the
2899  /// ASTContext is destroyed.
2900  ///
2901  /// \param Callback A callback function that will be invoked on destruction.
2902  ///
2903  /// \param Data Pointer data that will be provided to the callback function
2904  /// when it is called.
2905  void AddDeallocation(void (*Callback)(void *), void *Data) const;
2906 
2907  /// If T isn't trivially destructible, calls AddDeallocation to register it
2908  /// for destruction.
2909  template <typename T> void addDestruction(T *Ptr) const {
2910  if (!std::is_trivially_destructible<T>::value) {
2911  auto DestroyPtr = [](void *V) { static_cast<T *>(V)->~T(); };
2912  AddDeallocation(DestroyPtr, Ptr);
2913  }
2914  }
2915 
2916  GVALinkage GetGVALinkageForFunction(const FunctionDecl *FD) const;
2917  GVALinkage GetGVALinkageForVariable(const VarDecl *VD);
2918 
2919  /// Determines if the decl can be CodeGen'ed or deserialized from PCH
2920  /// lazily, only when used; this is only relevant for function or file scoped
2921  /// var definitions.
2922  ///
2923  /// \returns true if the function/var must be CodeGen'ed/deserialized even if
2924  /// it is not used.
2925  bool DeclMustBeEmitted(const Decl *D);
2926 
2927  /// Visits all versions of a multiversioned function with the passed
2928  /// predicate.
2929  void forEachMultiversionedFunctionVersion(
2930  const FunctionDecl *FD,
2931  llvm::function_ref<void(FunctionDecl *)> Pred) const;
2932 
2933  const CXXConstructorDecl *
2934  getCopyConstructorForExceptionObject(CXXRecordDecl *RD);
2935 
2936  void addCopyConstructorForExceptionObject(CXXRecordDecl *RD,
2937  CXXConstructorDecl *CD);
2938 
2939  void addTypedefNameForUnnamedTagDecl(TagDecl *TD, TypedefNameDecl *TND);
2940 
2941  TypedefNameDecl *getTypedefNameForUnnamedTagDecl(const TagDecl *TD);
2942 
2943  void addDeclaratorForUnnamedTagDecl(TagDecl *TD, DeclaratorDecl *DD);
2944 
2945  DeclaratorDecl *getDeclaratorForUnnamedTagDecl(const TagDecl *TD);
2946 
2947  void setManglingNumber(const NamedDecl *ND, unsigned Number);
2948  unsigned getManglingNumber(const NamedDecl *ND) const;
2949 
2950  void setStaticLocalNumber(const VarDecl *VD, unsigned Number);
2951  unsigned getStaticLocalNumber(const VarDecl *VD) const;
2952 
2953  /// Retrieve the context for computing mangling numbers in the given
2954  /// DeclContext.
2955  MangleNumberingContext &getManglingNumberContext(const DeclContext *DC);
2956  enum NeedExtraManglingDecl_t { NeedExtraManglingDecl };
2957  MangleNumberingContext &getManglingNumberContext(NeedExtraManglingDecl_t,
2958  const Decl *D);
2959 
2960  std::unique_ptr<MangleNumberingContext> createMangleNumberingContext() const;
2961 
2962  /// Used by ParmVarDecl to store on the side the
2963  /// index of the parameter when it exceeds the size of the normal bitfield.
2964  void setParameterIndex(const ParmVarDecl *D, unsigned index);
2965 
2966  /// Used by ParmVarDecl to retrieve on the side the
2967  /// index of the parameter when it exceeds the size of the normal bitfield.
2968  unsigned getParameterIndex(const ParmVarDecl *D) const;
2969 
2970  /// Return a string representing the human readable name for the specified
2971  /// function declaration or file name. Used by SourceLocExpr and
2972  /// PredefinedExpr to cache evaluated results.
2973  StringLiteral *getPredefinedStringLiteralFromCache(StringRef Key) const;
2974 
2975  /// Return a declaration for the global GUID object representing the given
2976  /// GUID value.
2977  MSGuidDecl *getMSGuidDecl(MSGuidDeclParts Parts) const;
2978 
2979  /// Return the template parameter object of the given type with the given
2980  /// value.
2981  TemplateParamObjectDecl *getTemplateParamObjectDecl(QualType T,
2982  const APValue &V) const;
2983 
2984  /// Parses the target attributes passed in, and returns only the ones that are
2985  /// valid feature names.
2986  ParsedTargetAttr filterFunctionTargetAttrs(const TargetAttr *TD) const;
2987 
2988  void getFunctionFeatureMap(llvm::StringMap<bool> &FeatureMap,
2989  const FunctionDecl *) const;
2990  void getFunctionFeatureMap(llvm::StringMap<bool> &FeatureMap,
2991  GlobalDecl GD) const;

2993  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2994  // Statistics
2995  //===--------------------------------------------------------------------===//
2996 
2997  /// The number of implicitly-declared default constructors.
2998  unsigned NumImplicitDefaultConstructors = 0;
2999 
3000  /// The number of implicitly-declared default constructors for
3001  /// which declarations were built.
3002  unsigned NumImplicitDefaultConstructorsDeclared = 0;
3003 
3004  /// The number of implicitly-declared copy constructors.
3005  unsigned NumImplicitCopyConstructors = 0;
3006 
3007  /// The number of implicitly-declared copy constructors for
3008  /// which declarations were built.
3009  unsigned NumImplicitCopyConstructorsDeclared = 0;
3010 
3011  /// The number of implicitly-declared move constructors.
3012  unsigned NumImplicitMoveConstructors = 0;
3013 
3014  /// The number of implicitly-declared move constructors for
3015  /// which declarations were built.
3016  unsigned NumImplicitMoveConstructorsDeclared = 0;
3017 
3018  /// The number of implicitly-declared copy assignment operators.
3019  unsigned NumImplicitCopyAssignmentOperators = 0;
3020 
3021  /// The number of implicitly-declared copy assignment operators for
3022  /// which declarations were built.
3023  unsigned NumImplicitCopyAssignmentOperatorsDeclared = 0;
3024 
3025  /// The number of implicitly-declared move assignment operators.
3026  unsigned NumImplicitMoveAssignmentOperators = 0;
3027 
3028  /// The number of implicitly-declared move assignment operators for
3029  /// which declarations were built.
3030  unsigned NumImplicitMoveAssignmentOperatorsDeclared = 0;
3031 
3032  /// The number of implicitly-declared destructors.
3033  unsigned NumImplicitDestructors = 0;
3034 
3035  /// The number of implicitly-declared destructors for which
3036  /// declarations were built.
3037  unsigned NumImplicitDestructorsDeclared = 0;
3038 
3039 public:
3040  /// Initialize built-in types.
3041  ///
3042  /// This routine may only be invoked once for a given ASTContext object.
3043  /// It is normally invoked after ASTContext construction.
3044  ///
3045  /// \param Target The target
3046  void InitBuiltinTypes(const TargetInfo &Target,
3047  const TargetInfo *AuxTarget = nullptr);
3048 
3049 private:
3050  void InitBuiltinType(CanQualType &R, BuiltinType::Kind K);
3051 
3052  class ObjCEncOptions {
3053  unsigned Bits;
3054 
3055  ObjCEncOptions(unsigned Bits) : Bits(Bits) {}
3056 
3057  public:
3058  ObjCEncOptions() : Bits(0) {}
3059  ObjCEncOptions(const ObjCEncOptions &RHS) : Bits(RHS.Bits) {}
3060 
3061 #define OPT_LIST(V) \
3062  V(ExpandPointedToStructures, 0) \
3063  V(ExpandStructures, 1) \
3064  V(IsOutermostType, 2) \
3065  V(EncodingProperty, 3) \
3066  V(IsStructField, 4) \
3067  V(EncodeBlockParameters, 5) \
3068  V(EncodeClassNames, 6) \
3069 
3070 #define V(N,I) ObjCEncOptions& set##N() { Bits |= 1 << I; return *this; }
3071 OPT_LIST(V)
3072 #undef V
3073 
3074 #define V(N,I) bool N() const { return Bits & 1 << I; }
3075 OPT_LIST(V)
3076 #undef V
3077 
3078 #undef OPT_LIST
3079 
3080  LLVM_NODISCARD ObjCEncOptions keepingOnly(ObjCEncOptions Mask) const {
3081  return Bits & Mask.Bits;
3082  }
3083 
3084  LLVM_NODISCARD ObjCEncOptions forComponentType() const {
3085  ObjCEncOptions Mask = ObjCEncOptions()
3086  .setIsOutermostType()
3087  .setIsStructField();
3088  return Bits & ~Mask.Bits;
3089  }
3090  };
3091 
3092  // Return the Objective-C type encoding for a given type.
3093  void getObjCEncodingForTypeImpl(QualType t, std::string &S,
3094  ObjCEncOptions Options,
3095  const FieldDecl *Field,
3096  QualType *NotEncodedT = nullptr) const;
3097 
3098  // Adds the encoding of the structure's members.
3099  void getObjCEncodingForStructureImpl(RecordDecl *RD, std::string &S,
3100  const FieldDecl *Field,
3101  bool includeVBases = true,
3102  QualType *NotEncodedT=nullptr) const;
3103 
3104 public:
3105  // Adds the encoding of a method parameter or return type.
3106  void getObjCEncodingForMethodParameter(Decl::ObjCDeclQualifier QT,
3107  QualType T, std::string& S,
3108  bool Extended) const;
3109 
3110  /// Returns true if this is an inline-initialized static data member
3111  /// which is treated as a definition for MSVC compatibility.
3112  bool isMSStaticDataMemberInlineDefinition(const VarDecl *VD) const;
3113 
3114  enum class InlineVariableDefinitionKind {
3115  /// Not an inline variable.
3116  None,
3117 
3118  /// Weak definition of inline variable.
3119  Weak,
3120 
3121  /// Weak for now, might become strong later in this TU.
3122  WeakUnknown,
3123 
3124  /// Strong definition.
3125  Strong
3126  };
3127 
3128  /// Determine whether a definition of this inline variable should
3129  /// be treated as a weak or strong definition. For compatibility with
3130  /// C++14 and before, for a constexpr static data member, if there is an
3131  /// out-of-line declaration of the member, we may promote it from weak to
3132  /// strong.
3133  InlineVariableDefinitionKind
3134  getInlineVariableDefinitionKind(const VarDecl *VD) const;
3135 
3136 private:
3137  friend class DeclarationNameTable;
3138  friend class DeclContext;
3139 
3140  const ASTRecordLayout &
3141  getObjCLayout(const ObjCInterfaceDecl *D,
3142  const ObjCImplementationDecl *Impl) const;
3143 
3144  /// A set of deallocations that should be performed when the
3145  /// ASTContext is destroyed.
3146  // FIXME: We really should have a better mechanism in the ASTContext to
3147  // manage running destructors for types which do variable sized allocation
3148  // within the AST. In some places we thread the AST bump pointer allocator
3149  // into the datastructures which avoids this mess during deallocation but is
3150  // wasteful of memory, and here we require a lot of error prone book keeping
3151  // in order to track and run destructors while we're tearing things down.
3152  using DeallocationFunctionsAndArguments =
3153  llvm::SmallVector<std::pair<void (*)(void *), void *>, 16>;
3154  mutable DeallocationFunctionsAndArguments Deallocations;
3155 
3156  // FIXME: This currently contains the set of StoredDeclMaps used
3157  // by DeclContext objects. This probably should not be in ASTContext,
3158  // but we include it here so that ASTContext can quickly deallocate them.
3159  llvm::PointerIntPair<StoredDeclsMap *, 1> LastSDM;
3160 
3161  std::vector<Decl *> TraversalScope;
3162 
3163  std::unique_ptr<VTableContextBase> VTContext;
3164 
3165  void ReleaseDeclContextMaps();
3166 
3167 public:
3168  enum PragmaSectionFlag : unsigned {
3169  PSF_None = 0,
3170  PSF_Read = 0x1,
3171  PSF_Write = 0x2,
3172  PSF_Execute = 0x4,
3173  PSF_Implicit = 0x8,
3174  PSF_ZeroInit = 0x10,
3175  PSF_Invalid = 0x80000000U,
3176  };
3177 
3178  struct SectionInfo {
3179  NamedDecl *Decl;
3180  SourceLocation PragmaSectionLocation;
3181  int SectionFlags;
3182 
3183  SectionInfo() = default;
3184  SectionInfo(NamedDecl *Decl, SourceLocation PragmaSectionLocation,
3185  int SectionFlags)
3186  : Decl(Decl), PragmaSectionLocation(PragmaSectionLocation),
3187  SectionFlags(SectionFlags) {}
3188  };
3189 
3190  llvm::StringMap<SectionInfo> SectionInfos;
3191 
3192  /// Return a new OMPTraitInfo object owned by this context.
3193  OMPTraitInfo &getNewOMPTraitInfo();
3194 
3195  /// Whether a C++ static variable may be externalized.
3196  bool mayExternalizeStaticVar(const Decl *D) const;
3197 
3198  /// Whether a C++ static variable should be externalized.
3199  bool shouldExternalizeStaticVar(const Decl *D) const;
3200 
3201  StringRef getCUIDHash() const;
3202 
3203  void AddSYCLKernelNamingDecl(const CXXRecordDecl *RD);
3204  bool IsSYCLKernelNamingDecl(const NamedDecl *RD) const;
3205  unsigned GetSYCLKernelNamingIndex(const NamedDecl *RD);
3206  /// A SourceLocation to store whether we have evaluated a kernel name already,
3207  /// and where it happened. If so, we need to diagnose an illegal use of the
3208  /// builtin. This should only contain SYCLUniqueStableNameExprs and
3209  /// SYCLUniqueStableIdExprs.
3210  llvm::MapVector<const Expr *, std::string>
3211  SYCLUniqueStableNameEvaluatedValues;
3212 
3213 private:
3214  /// All OMPTraitInfo objects live in this collection, one per
3215  /// `pragma omp [begin] declare variant` directive.
3216  SmallVector<std::unique_ptr<OMPTraitInfo>, 4> OMPTraitInfoVector;
3217 
3218  /// A list of the (right now just lambda decls) declarations required to
3219  /// name all the SYCL kernels in the translation unit, so that we can get the
3220  /// correct kernel name, as well as implement
3221  /// __builtin_sycl_unique_stable_name.
3222  llvm::DenseMap<const DeclContext *,
3223  llvm::SmallPtrSet<const CXXRecordDecl *, 4>>
3224  SYCLKernelNamingTypes;
3225  std::unique_ptr<ItaniumMangleContext> SYCLKernelFilterContext;
3226  void FilterSYCLKernelNamingDecls(
3227  const CXXRecordDecl *RD,
3228  llvm::SmallVectorImpl<const CXXRecordDecl *> &Decls);
3229 };
3230 
3231 /// Insertion operator for diagnostics.
3232 const StreamingDiagnostic &operator<<(const StreamingDiagnostic &DB,
3233  const ASTContext::SectionInfo &Section);
3234 
3235 /// Utility function for constructing a nullary selector.
3236 inline Selector GetNullarySelector(StringRef name, ASTContext &Ctx) {
3237  IdentifierInfo* II = &Ctx.Idents.get(name);
3238  return Ctx.Selectors.getSelector(0, &II);
3239 }
3240 
3241 /// Utility function for constructing an unary selector.
3242 inline Selector GetUnarySelector(StringRef name, ASTContext &Ctx) {
3243  IdentifierInfo* II = &Ctx.Idents.get(name);
3244  return Ctx.Selectors.getSelector(1, &II);
3245 }
3246 
3247 } // namespace clang
3248 
3249 // operator new and delete aren't allowed inside namespaces.
3250 
3251 /// Placement new for using the ASTContext's allocator.
3252 ///
3253 /// This placement form of operator new uses the ASTContext's allocator for
3254 /// obtaining memory.
3255 ///
3256 /// IMPORTANT: These are also declared in clang/AST/ASTContextAllocate.h!
3257 /// Any changes here need to also be made there.
3258 ///
3259 /// We intentionally avoid using a nothrow specification here so that the calls
3260 /// to this operator will not perform a null check on the result -- the
3261 /// underlying allocator never returns null pointers.
3262 ///
3263 /// Usage looks like this (assuming there's an ASTContext 'Context' in scope):
3264 /// @code
3265 /// // Default alignment (8)
3266 /// IntegerLiteral *Ex = new (Context) IntegerLiteral(arguments);
3267 /// // Specific alignment
3268 /// IntegerLiteral *Ex2 = new (Context, 4) IntegerLiteral(arguments);
3269 /// @endcode
3270 /// Memory allocated through this placement new operator does not need to be
3271 /// explicitly freed, as ASTContext will free all of this memory when it gets
3272 /// destroyed. Please note that you cannot use delete on the pointer.
3273 ///
3274 /// @param Bytes The number of bytes to allocate. Calculated by the compiler.
3275 /// @param C The ASTContext that provides the allocator.
3276 /// @param Alignment The alignment of the allocated memory (if the underlying
3277 /// allocator supports it).
3278 /// @return The allocated memory. Could be nullptr.
3279 inline void *operator new(size_t Bytes, const clang::ASTContext &C,
3280  size_t Alignment /* = 8 */) {
3281  return C.Allocate(Bytes, Alignment);
3282 }
3283 
3284 /// Placement delete companion to the new above.
3285 ///
3286 /// This operator is just a companion to the new above. There is no way of
3287 /// invoking it directly; see the new operator for more details. This operator
3288 /// is called implicitly by the compiler if a placement new expression using
3289 /// the ASTContext throws in the object constructor.
3290 inline void operator delete(void *Ptr, const clang::ASTContext &C, size_t) {
3291  C.Deallocate(Ptr);
3292 }
3293 
3294 /// This placement form of operator new[] uses the ASTContext's allocator for
3295 /// obtaining memory.
3296 ///
3297 /// We intentionally avoid using a nothrow specification here so that the calls
3298 /// to this operator will not perform a null check on the result -- the
3299 /// underlying allocator never returns null pointers.
3300 ///
3301 /// Usage looks like this (assuming there's an ASTContext 'Context' in scope):
3302 /// @code
3303 /// // Default alignment (8)
3304 /// char *data = new (Context) char[10];
3305 /// // Specific alignment
3306 /// char *data = new (Context, 4) char[10];
3307 /// @endcode
3308 /// Memory allocated through this placement new[] operator does not need to be
3309 /// explicitly freed, as ASTContext will free all of this memory when it gets
3310 /// destroyed. Please note that you cannot use delete on the pointer.
3311 ///
3312 /// @param Bytes The number of bytes to allocate. Calculated by the compiler.
3313 /// @param C The ASTContext that provides the allocator.
3314 /// @param Alignment The alignment of the allocated memory (if the underlying
3315 /// allocator supports it).
3316 /// @return The allocated memory. Could be nullptr.
3317 inline void *operator new[](size_t Bytes, const clang::ASTContext& C,
3318  size_t Alignment /* = 8 */) {
3319  return C.Allocate(Bytes, Alignment);
3320 }
3321 
3322 /// Placement delete[] companion to the new[] above.
3323 ///
3324 /// This operator is just a companion to the new[] above. There is no way of
3325 /// invoking it directly; see the new[] operator for more details. This operator
3326 /// is called implicitly by the compiler if a placement new[] expression using
3327 /// the ASTContext throws in the object constructor.
3328 inline void operator delete[](void *Ptr, const clang::ASTContext &C, size_t) {
3329  C.Deallocate(Ptr);
3330 }
3331 
3332 /// Create the representation of a LazyGenerationalUpdatePtr.
3333 template <typename Owner, typename T,
3334  void (clang::ExternalASTSource::*Update)(Owner)>
3335 typename clang::LazyGenerationalUpdatePtr<Owner, T, Update>::ValueType
3336  clang::LazyGenerationalUpdatePtr<Owner, T, Update>::makeValue(
3337  const clang::ASTContext &Ctx, T Value) {
3338  // Note, this is implemented here so that ExternalASTSource.h doesn't need to
3339  // include ASTContext.h. We explicitly instantiate it for all relevant types
3340  // in ASTContext.cpp.
3341  if (auto *Source = Ctx.getExternalSource())
3342  return new (Ctx) LazyData(Source, Value);
3343  return Value;
3344 }
3345
